**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 191 **Complexity** Simple Hurricane-force winds rip through the area as [Agyra](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3406) flies past. --- **Stealth** DC 27 (expert) to note stirrings in the wind **Disable** DC 27 [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) (expert) or [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) (expert) to crouch and contort out of the wind's way (disables the hazard for only yourself), DC 30 [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) to point out a safe space from the winds for up to 3 creatures **Hurricane Flyby** ([air](/Traits.aspx?ID=527)) **Trigger** Agyra flies overhead for at least 120 feet in a straight line; **Effect** Supernaturally powerful winds batter the area in a line that is 120 feet long and 30 feet wide and 30 feet tall. Each creature in the line must attempt a DC 29 Reflex save. Moving above ground against the direction of the line is [greater difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=453) for 1 minute. **Critical Success** The creature is [pushed](/Rules.aspx?ID=451) 10 feet along the line.
**Success** The creature takes 5d6 bludgeoning damage and is pushed 15 along the line.
**Failure** The creatures takes 10d6 bludgeoning damage, is pushed 30 feet along the line, and is knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88).
**Critical Failure** The creature takes 20d6 bludgeoning damage, is pushed 45 feet along the line, is knocked prone, and is [stunned 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=93) for 1 round.If the line overlaps a body of water, the winds cause massive waves that deal 6d6 bludgeoning damage to creatures in the water or within 15 feet of the waterline (DC 29 basic Reflex save). On a critical failure, a creature is also swept 30 feet away from the waterline and 15 feet beneath the water's surface.
name: Wind Surge

Four successes are needed to strengthen the area enough against the instability to end the hazard. **Bone-Shattering Rhythms** **Trigger** Three or more characters enter the area of planar instability; **Effect** Pent-up magical energy from another world begins to release in waves of overwhelming sound. The hazard rolls initiative. --- **Routine** (1 action) Each creature in the area must attempt a DC 36 Fortitude save. A creature with the [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) condition from this hazard takes a –2 circumstance penalty on further saves against this effect.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature takes 2d6+6 sonic damage and is [deafened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=66) for 1 round.
**Failure** The creature takes 4d6+13 sonic damage and is slowed 1 for 2 rounds.
**Critical Failure** The creature takes 8d6+26 sonic damage and is slowed 2 for 2 rounds. --- **Reset** The area becomes unstable again after 24 hours.

<title level="1" right="Hazard 14" pfs="">[Boneburst](/Hazards.aspx?ID=464)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=683" />
<trait label="Complex" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=30" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
<trait label="Unholy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=521" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 209

**Complexity** Complex

</column>

A low howl rises from the ground, gathering strength as scraps of ancient armor, bone fragments, and clumps of earth rise into the air. Crimson vapor holds the faceless shape together, never coalescing into an identifiable form, but merely writhing through ever-changing images of torment and destruction.

---

**Stealth**
+38 (master) to notice that clouds of debris are coalescing into swirling, faceless shapes held together by rust-red fog and spectral, dripping clots of gore

**Disable**
DC 38 [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) (master) to temporarily negate the psychic resonances around each fragment, causing the agglomeration to fall apart, or DC 33 [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) (expert) to temporarily interrupt [Verex-That-Was's](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3409) unholy power with divine energy from another source. If a boneburst takes 40 points of damage from a single source and it isn't destroyed, it is rendered dormant for 1 round.

<column gap="tiny">

**AC** 33, **Fort** +30, **Ref** +28

**Hardness**
20

**HP**
90 (BT 45)

**Immunities**
critical hits, object immunities, precision damage

</column>

**Gathering Mist** <actions string="Reaction" /> **Trigger** A living creature approaches within 50 feet of a dormant boneburst; **Effect** The boneburst awakens, roiling up from the ground, and rolls initiative.

---
**Routine**
(2 actions) An awakened boneburst occupies a 20- foot square; it is reduced to a 10-foot square when it reaches its BT. It can occupy the same space as other creatures, but not other bonebursts. On its initiative, the boneburst [Flies](/Rules.aspx?ID=2312) up to 30 feet toward the nearest living creature as its first action. As its second action, the boneburst then coalesces around any living creature within its space, invisible shards of bone tearing at their flesh. Each living creature in its space takes 2d10+18 slashing damage and 1d10 spirit damage (DC 35 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). If no living creatures are within its space, the boneburst can use its second action to Fly up to 30 feet.

---
**Reset**
If the boneburst spends 5 consecutive rounds without coalescing around a living creature, it falls dormant, sinking back into the earth. After it falls dormant or is disabled, the boneburst gathers energy over the course of the next hour, after which it can be triggered again.
name: Boneburst

<title level="1" right="Hazard 23" pfs="">[Armageddon Orb](/Hazards.aspx?ID=465)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Trap" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=158" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 100

**Complexity** Simple

</column>

A roiling red orb, forged from a drop of the god [Rovagug](/Deities.aspx?ID=291)’s blood, rains fire from the sky when a specified condition is met.

---

**Stealth**
DC 10 or [_detect magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485)

**Disable**
DC 48 [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) (legendary) to imbue a [thieves’ toolkit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2758) with aspects representing [Asmodeus](/Deities.aspx?ID=278) and [Sarenrae](/Deities.aspx?ID=292) and use them to drain away the orb’s power over 10 minutes; the character attempting this check takes 5 fire damage each round until the orb is depleted

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

**Burn It All** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([death](/Traits.aspx?ID=571), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604)) **Trigger** A special condition set by the trap’s creator occurs, typically the event of their death; **Effect** Fire rains from the sky in a 100-mile radius, dealing 10d6 fire damage to creatures and objects in the area. Each creature or object can attempt a DC 46 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2247) Reflex save. Any creature reduced to 0 Hit Points by this damage dies instantly. This is not enough damage to completely burn away a forest or level an entire mountain or city, but it typically kills most creatures in the area.

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Mechanical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=105" />
<trait label="Trap" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=158" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 101

**Complexity** Simple

</column>

An iron trapdoor covers an infinitely deep 10-footsquare pit.

---

**Stealth**
DC 30 (or 0 if the trapdoor is disabled or broken) or [_detect magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485)

**Disable**
DC 28 [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) (trained) to remove the trapdoor

<column gap="tiny">

**AC** 28, **Fort** +12, **Ref** +12

**Trapdoor Hardness**
9

**Trapdoor HP**
36 (BT 18)

**Immunities**
[critical hits](/Rules.aspx?ID=2190), [object immunities](/Rules.aspx?ID=2161), precision damage

</column>

**Infinite Pitfall** <actions string="Reaction" /> **Trigger** A creature walks onto the trapdoor; **Effect** The triggering creature falls in and continues to fall, potentially forever. That creature can try to [Grab an Edge](/Actions.aspx?ID=2313) to avoid falling. The DC to [Climb](/Actions.aspx?ID=2374) the walls or Grab an Edge is 26.

The pit contains many handholds, so the falling creature can try to Grab an Edge again every 6 seconds. If the creature succeeds, it can start to Climb out from that point (though it might be a very long climb, depending on how far the creature fell). Since the creature falls endlessly, it can rest and even prepare spells while falling, though items dropped while falling are usually lost forever.

---
**Reset**
The trap still causes creatures to fall forever if they fall in, but the trapdoor must be reset manually for the trap to become hidden again.
name: Bottomless Pit

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 102 **Complexity** Simple A choir of lost souls rises out of the floor, singing an eerie chant that terrifies its listeners and buffets their bodies with walls of sound. --- **Stealth** DC 20 (expert) **Disable** DC 28 [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) (trained) to disrupt the song’s resonance with another tune or DC 28 [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) (trained) to ritually silence the spirits **Profane Chant** ([auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) **Trigger** A creature [moves](/Traits.aspx?ID=658) within 10 feet of the section of floor from which the choir can arise; **Effect** The choir rises, and its song deals 4d8+18 mental damage to living creatures within 30 feet of the souls' spectral forms. Affected creatures must each attempt a DC 24 Will save. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature takes half damage and becomes [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 1.
**Failure** The creature takes full damage and becomes frightened 2.
**Critical Failure** The creature takes double damage. It also becomes frightened 3 and [fleeing](/Conditions.aspx?ID=74) for 1 round.
name: Ghostly Choir

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 102 **Complexity** Simple An enormous hammer at an edifice’s entrance swings down in an attempt to damage a creature entering an area, push it back, and prevent it from going any further. --- **Stealth** DC 30 (expert) **Disable** DC 28 [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) (expert) once on the hammer itself and once on its joint to prevent the hammer from swinging **AC** 32, **Fort** +24, **Ref** +15 **Hammer Hardness** 22 **Hamer HP** 88 (BT 44); **Joint Hardness** 16; **Joint HP** 64 (BT 32) **Immunities** [critical hits](/Rules.aspx?ID=2190), [object immunities](/Rules.aspx?ID=2161), precision damage **Forbid Entry** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) **Trigger** A creature attempts to enter through the entrance; **Effect** The hammer swings down, making a [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) against the triggering creature.
**Melee** hammer +28, **Damage** 6d8+20 bludgeoning plus the target is knocked back 10 feet and must succeed at a DC 30 Will save or be unable to enter the edifice through any entrance for 24 hours (on a critical hit, the target automatically fails the Will save); no multiple attack penalty --- **Reset** The trap resets over the course of the round, and is ready to swing again 1 round later.
name: Hammer Of Forbiddance

Semitransparent sheets of webbing span the entryway, ready to capture small insects or hamper larger creatures that pass through.

---

**Stealth**
DC 18 (expert)

**Disable**
DC 17 [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) (trained) to dislodge it

<column gap="tiny">

**AC** 19, **Fort** +10, **Ref** +11

**HP**
26 (BT 13)

**Immunities**
[critical hits](/Rules.aspx?ID=2190), [object immunities](/Rules.aspx?ID=2161), precision damage

</column>

**Ensnare** <actions string="Reaction" /> **Trigger** A creature that isn’t a spider walks into the web; **Effect** The web wraps around the triggering creature’s body, clinging to their limbs. The triggering creature must succeed at a DC 20 Reflex save or take a –10-foot circumstance penalty to all their Speeds until they [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296) the web (DC 20). On a critical failure, the webbing also clings to the creature’s face, making them [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) 1, and they can’t attempt to reduce this condition until they Escape the web.
name: Hampering Web

<column gap="tiny">

**Curse the Intruders** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=566), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) **Trigger** The seal on the tomb is broken from the outside; **Effect** Each living creature within 60 feet must succeed at a DC 23 Will save or be subjected to the pharaoh’s curse. A cursed creature takes a –2 status penalty to Fortitude saves, and any natural or magical [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623) it receives is halved. **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 103 **Complexity** Simple This grotesque fungus releases poisonous spores when a creature comes near. --- **Stealth** DC 21 (trained) **Disable** DC 18 [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) (trained) to safely remove the mold **AC** 18, **Fort** +11, **Ref** +5 **HP** 30 (BT 15) **Immunities** [critical hits](/Rules.aspx?ID=2190), [object immunities](/Rules.aspx?ID=2161), precision damage **Weaknesses** [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) 10 **Feed on Decay** The mold expands when fed by blood or decay. If at any point a creature takes bleed damage while adjacent to the mold or a dying or dead body is adjacent to the mold, the mold grows to expand into every square adjacent to its current space. This can happen only once per day.
**Spore Burst** ([poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** A creature touches the mold, hits it, or damages it; **Effect** The mold explodes in a burst of spores and mold clusters, dealing 2d6+6 poison damage to creatures within 10 feet of it, with a DC 18 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2247) Fortitude save. --- **Reset** Unless entirely eradicated (typically with [acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=524) or fire), the mold regrows over the course of 2 weeks.
name: Poisonous Mold

<title level="1" right="Hazard 1" pfs="">[Slamming Door](/Hazards.aspx?ID=481)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Mechanical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=105" />
<trait label="Trap" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=158" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 104

**Complexity** Simple

</column>

Pressure-sensitive panels in the floor connect to a stone slab hidden in a hallway’s ceiling.

---

**Stealth**
DC 17 (trained)

**Disable**
DC 15 [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) (trained) on the floor panels before the slab falls

<column gap="tiny">

**AC** 16, **Fort** +10, **Ref** +2

**Hardness**
5

**HP**
20 (BT 10)

**Immunities**
[critical hits](/Rules.aspx?ID=2190), [object immunities](/Rules.aspx?ID=2161), precision damage

</column>

**Slam Shut** <actions string="Reaction" /> **Trigger** Pressure is placed on any floor tile; **Effect** The door falls, closing off the hallway. The stone slab deals 3d8 bludgeoning damage to anyone beneath or adjacent to the slab when it drops and pushes them out of its space in a random direction. A creature that succeeds at a DC 17 Reflex save takes no damage and rolls out of the way in a random direction. On a critical success, they can choose the direction. 

Lifting the fallen slab requires a successful DC 25 [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) check. Hitting the floor panels triggers the trap. The slab uses the same AC and saves as the trap, but it has Hardness 12, HP 48 (BT 24).
name: Slamming Door

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 104

**Complexity** Simple

</column>

Loose snow and ice have built up on a high surface, such as a tree branch or a rooftop. Its grip on the surface is tenuous, and it is likely to fall if the surface moves.

---

**Stealth**
DC 16 (trained)

**Disable**
DC 19 [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) (trained) to safely dislodge the snow, or deal any amount of [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) damage to destroy the hazard without triggering it

<column gap="tiny">

**AC** 18, **Fort** +11, **Ref** +3

**HP**
8

**Immunities**
[critical hits](/Rules.aspx?ID=2190), [object immunities](/Rules.aspx?ID=2161), precision damage

</column>

**Snowfall** <actions string="Reaction" /> **Trigger** A creature moves beneath where the snowfall is resting; **Effect** Ice and snow fall on the triggering creature, dealing 2d6+3 bludgeoning damage (DC 18 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save) and soaking their clothing. Until they change into fresh clothing or spend at least an hour in an area of normal or higher temperature, they treat [cold environments](/Rules.aspx?ID=2820) as one step colder (for example, mild cold as severe cold).
name: Snowfall

<title level="1" right="Hazard 5" pfs="">[Spectral Reflection](/Hazards.aspx?ID=484)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Haunt" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=88" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 104

**Complexity** Simple

</column>

The reflection in the mirror subtly twists and distorts, its expression taking on an unnerving sneer of malice.

---

**Stealth**
DC 26 (expert)

**Disable**
DC 23 [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) (trained) to exorcise the spirit, or DC 23 [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) (trained) to quickly cover the mirror

<column gap="tiny">

**AC** 19, **Fort** +15, **Ref** +10

**Hardness**
13

**HP**
50 (BT 25)

**Immunities**
[critical hits](/Rules.aspx?ID=2190), [object immunities](/Rules.aspx?ID=2161), precision damage

</column>

**Spectral Impale** <actions string="Reaction" /> **Trigger** A living creature approaches within 15 feet of the mirror, and the mirror is lit with [dim](/Rules.aspx?ID=2403) or brighter [light](/Rules.aspx?ID=2401); **Effect** Shadowy barbs impale the body of the reflected creature as the haunt makes a shadow barbs [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306). 

**Melee** shadow barbs +19, **Damage** 4d8+9 [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510) plus sap vitality 

**Sap Vitality** A creature hit by the reflection’s shadow barbs must attempt a DC 22 Fortitude save as the haunt tries to draw a portion of its vital essence into the mirror. The target is [drained 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) on a failed saving throw, or drained 2 on a critical failure.

---
**Reset**
The haunt re-forms after 1 minute, manifesting in any mirror within its infused area (see Special Below). 

**Special** Spectral reflections often infuse entire buildings, manifesting in any sufficiently large mirror. The example Hit Points and Hardness given represent a typical mirror reinforced by the haunt; at your discretion, the haunt might appear in reflective surfaces that are harder to destroy. Defeating a manifestation through damage destroys the surface, preventing the haunt from using it again. If this haunt appears in an area with many mirrors, consider giving PCs double or even triple the XP for a typical simple hazard.
name: Spectral Reflection

<title level="1" right="Hazard 4" pfs="">[Titanic Flytrap](/Hazards.aspx?ID=485)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Environmental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=62" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 105

**Complexity** Simple

</column>

On the surface, a titanic flytrap appears to be a patch of the more common flytrap plant, but beneath murky waters it hides a far larger set of jaws, reaching 10 feet across, reinforced with woody branches and lined with paralytic hairs.

---

**Stealth**
DC 25 (trained)

**Disable**
DC 22 [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) (trained) to mislead the flytrap’s sense of weight and pressure

<column gap="tiny">

**AC** 21, **Fort** +15, **Ref** +8

**HP**
56 (BT 28)

**Immunities**
[mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)

**Resistances**
[acid 20](/Traits.aspx?ID=524), [fire 10](/Traits.aspx?ID=604)

</column>

**Snap Shut** <actions string="Reaction" /> **Trigger** A Small or Medium creature moves into a square that is within reach of the flytrap’s hidden jaws; **Effect** The flytrap’s jaws snap shut, making a jaws [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) against the triggering creature.

**Melee** <actions string="Single Action" /> jaws +17, **Effect** devour 

**Devour** The target is trapped by the flytrap’s jaws, gaining the [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) condition until it [Escapes](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296) (DC 21). Additionally, it is exposed to the titanic flytrap toxin from the hundreds of tiny hairs that line the inside of its leaves. If the flytrap’s jaws Strike was a critical success, the target takes a –2 circumstance penalty to its saving throws against this poison. At the end of each of the target’s turns that it remains grabbed, the target takes 3d6 [acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=524) damage.

**Titanic Flytrap Toxin** ([contact](/Traits.aspx?ID=565), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669)) **Saving Throw** DC 21 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 4 rounds; **Stage 1** 2d6 poison damage and [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=93) 1 (1 round); **Stage 2** 3d6 poison damage and stunned 2 (1 round); **Stage 3** 4d6 poison damage and [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85) (1 round)

---
**Reset**
1 hour (or longer, after a large meal)
name: Titanic Flytrap

<title level="1" right="Hazard 19" pfs="">[Vorpal Executioner](/Hazards.aspx?ID=486)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Mechanical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=105" />
<trait label="Trap" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=158" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 105

**Complexity** Simple

</column>

A wickedly sharp saw blade descends and travels along grooves in a complex path throughout the room, attempting to decapitate everyone within.

---

**Stealth**
DC 43 (expert)

**Disable**
DC 41 [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) (expert) at four different junctions to jam all the saw blade’s possible paths, preventing it from traveling through the room

<column gap="tiny">

**AC** 43, **Fort** +32, **Ref** +32

**Hardness**
30

**HP**
120 (BT 60) per junction

**Immunities**
[critical hits](/Rules.aspx?ID=2190), [object immunities](/Rules.aspx?ID=2161), precision damage

</column>

**Total Decapitation** <actions string="Reaction" /> **Trigger** A creature attempts to exit the room; **Effect** The saw blade travels along its path, making one [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) against each creature in the room, twisting and varying its height for a maximum chance of beheading its targets.

**Melee** saw blade +40 ([deadly d12](/Traits.aspx?ID=570), [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=571)), **Damage** 6d12+25 slashing plus decapitation; no multiple attack penalty 

**Decapitation** On a critical hit, a target must succeed at a DC 39 Fortitude save or be decapitated, dying instantly unless it can survive without a head

---
**Reset**
The trap resets over the course of the round and can be triggered again 1 round later.
name: Vorpal Executioner

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 105 **Complexity** Complex Allies appear to shed their disguises and reveal themselves to be malevolent monsters. --- **Stealth** +21 (expert) **Disable** DC 28 [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) (expert) twice to confound the haunt with your own deceptions, or DC 28 [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) (trained) twice to create a ward against the haunt’s mental influence **Unmask** ([illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=629), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) **Trigger** Two or more creatures enter the haunt’s area; **Effect** Each creature sees the forms of nearby creatures shift and change, appearing to transform into fiendish or aberrant beings with a thirst for blood. Each creature in the area must attempt a DC 30 Will save. The haunt then rolls initiative. **Critical Success** The creature sees through the illusions entirely and is temporarily immune to the haunt’s routine for 1 minute.
**Success** The creature is unaffected by the strange images.
**Failure** The creature believes the illusions to be true; if they become [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=63) by the haunt’s routine, they can’t attempt flat checks to end the confused condition when they take damage.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the creature is left with a lingering suspicion of others and can’t benefit from Aid reactions for 24 hours. --- **Routine** (1 action; [illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=629), [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) The haunt continues to confound victims’ senses and inspire them to commit violence against each other. Each creature in the haunt’s area must attempt a DC 26 Will save. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected and temporarily immune for 1 minute.
**Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Failure** The creature is [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=63) for 1 round.
**Critical Failure** The creature is confused for 1 minute. --- **Reset** The haunt deactivates 1 minute after all creatures leave the area but resets immediately thereafter
name: Confounding Betrayal

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 106 **Complexity** Complex An eerie orchestra compels all who hear it to dance until they collapse from exhaustion. --- **Stealth** +32 (master) **Disable** DC 42 [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) (expert) three times to frighten dancers and spectral musicians alike away from participating in the deadly performance, DC 40 [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) (master) twice to produce a tune discordant enough to disrupt the compulsion, or DC 42 [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) (master) three times to banish the spirits with prayers **Prelude** ([auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) **Trigger** A creature approaches within 30 feet of the orchestra; **Effect** The orchestra compels all creatures that can hear it to begin dancing. Each creature must attempt a DC 41 Will save, with the following effects. The haunt then rolls initiative. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) and cannot use reactions. Additionally, it must spend 1 of its actions each round dancing. Dancing is a [move](/Traits.aspx?ID=658) action that allows the creature to [Stride](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305) up to half its Speed.
**Failure** As success, except the creature must spend 2 of its actions each round dancing.
**Critical Failure** As failure, except the creature must spend 3 of its actions each round dancing. --- **Routine** (1 action; [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) The orchestra performs a raucous tune, compelling all creatures that can hear it to spend actions dancing. Each round, creature must attempt a DC 37 Will save; the results of this save modify the number of actions that the creature must spend dancing each round. If this would cause the creature to spend more actions dancing than it can use on its turn, the creature takes 10d6 damage (or double that on a critical failure) from moving faster than its body can manage. **Critical Success** The creature decreases the number of actions it must spend dancing by 1.
**Success** No effect.
**Failure** The creature increases the actions it must spend dancing by 1.
**Critical Failure** The creature increases the actions it must spend dancing by 2. --- **Reset** The eerie orchestra spends an hour retuning its phantasmal instruments, after which it is ready to begin its routine again.
name: Dance of Death

<title level="1" right="Hazard 3" pfs="">[Drowning Pit](/Hazards.aspx?ID=489)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Complex" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=30" />
<trait label="Mechanical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=105" />
<trait label="Trap" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=158" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 106

**Complexity** Complex

</column>

A trapdoor covers a 10-foot-square pit that’s 30 feet deep and has 5 feet of water at the bottom. Four water spouts in the walls connect to hidden water tanks. Each water spout extends out of a different wall, 6 inches from the top of the pit.

---

**Stealth**
+10 (trained); DC 22 (expert) to notice the water spouts once the pit opens

**Disable**
DC 18 [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) (trained) to seal each water spout, DC 22 Thievery (trained) to open the trapdoor, or DC 22 [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) to [Force Open](/Actions.aspx?ID=2375) the trapdoor

<column gap="tiny">

**AC** 19, **Fort** +8, **Ref** +5

**Trapdoor Hardness**
15

**Trapdoor HP**
60 (BT 30); **Spout Hardness** 8; **Spout** HP 32 (BT 16)

**Immunities**
[critical hits](/Rules.aspx?ID=2190), [object immunities](/Rules.aspx?ID=2161), precision damage

</column>

**Pitfall** <actions string="Reaction" /> **Trigger** A creature walks onto the trapdoor; **Effect** The triggering creature falls in and takes damage from the fall, reduced by 5 feet for falling into the water (typically 12 bludgeoning damage). A creature can [Grab an Edge](/Actions.aspx?ID=2313) to avoid falling. The trapdoor then slams shut, and the hazard rolls initiative.

---
**Routine**
(4 actions) The trap loses 1 action each turn for each disabled water spout. On each of the trap’s actions, a spout pours water, increasing the depth of the water by 5 feet. Once the pit is full of water, the pit stops using actions, but creatures in the pit begin [drowning](/Rules.aspx?ID=2439).

---
**Reset**
The trap can be reset if the door is manually reengaged and the water tanks are refilled; it can be reset without draining the pit, but doing so renders the pit less effective.
name: Drowning Pit

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 106 **Complexity** Complex A raging spectral inferno arises out of thin air, strengthening all [undead](/MonsterTemplates.aspx?ID=3) creatures within its area. This haunt most often arises from the charred remains of a group of three people who burned to death, whether in a terrible accident or a deliberate execution, and their unavenged souls burn with rage. --- **Stealth** +18 (expert) **Disable** DC 27 [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) (expert) to temporarily calm the rage of one of the three spirits, or DC 30 [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) (trained) to exorcise one of the spirits; three total successes are required to disable the haunt **Searing Agony** ([mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) **Trigger** A living creature approaches within 10 feet of the remains of a victim of the original fire; **Effect** Memories of the pain suffered by the fire’s past victims assault the triggering creature’s mind. The creature must attempt a DC 25 Will save, and the haunt then rolls initiative. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) 1.
**Failure** The creature is sickened 2.
**Critical Failure** The creature is sickened 3, and it is [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) for as long as it remains within the haunt’s area and for 3 rounds thereafter. --- **Routine** (1 action) Phantom flames rage across the haunted area, dealing 4d6 fire damage to each living creature within the area (DC 23 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Will save). [Undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=722) creatures in the area are infused with flames for the following round. They gain the [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) trait and [immunity](/Rules.aspx?ID=2312) to fire, and all their attacks deal an additional 1d6 fire damage. Objects in the area are unaffected. --- **Reset** The flames cease 1 minute after all living creatures leave the area, but after 1 hour, the anger and pain simmer up and the haunt is ready to trigger again.
name: Eternal Flame

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 107 **Complexity** Complex A whirling tornado of spectrally propelled glass and steel slices whatever it touches to ribbons. --- **Stealth** +25 (expert) **Disable** DC 35 [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) (master) to precisely adjust the blades so that they destroy each other, or DC 38 [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) (expert) to weaken the haunt; four successes are required to disable it **AC** 33, **Fort** +27, **Ref** +25, **Will** +22 **Hardness** 20 **HP** 100 (BT 50) **Immunities** [critical hits](/Rules.aspx?ID=2190), [object immunities](/Rules.aspx?ID=2161), precision damage **Weaknesses** [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509) 15 **Whirling Blades** **Trigger** Three or more creatures enter the area of the haunt; **Effect** Sharp fragments lift up from the ground and begin to spin in rapid circles taking up one 5-foot square. Each creature in its path must attempt a DC 33 Reflex save. **Success** The creature is unaffected.

**Critical Failure** The creature takes double damage and 1d10 persistent bleed damage. It also becomes [wounded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=99) 1 (or increases its wounded value by 1, if it is already wounded). --- **Reset** The haunt draws jagged shards back into its area over the course of an hour, after which it can trigger again.

<title level="1" right="Hazard 8" pfs="">[Poisoned Dart Gallery](/Hazards.aspx?ID=492)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Complex" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=30" />
<trait label="Mechanical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=105" />
<trait label="Trap" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=158" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 107

**Complexity** Complex

</column>

Countless holes to launch poison darts from line a long hallway with a hidden control panel on the far end.

---

**Stealth**
+16 (expert) or DC 31 (master) to notice the control panel

**Disable**
DC 21 [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) (expert) on the control panel deactivates the trap

<column gap="tiny">

**AC** 27, **Fort** +13, **Ref** +17

**Hardness**
14

**HP**
56 (BT 28)

**Immunities**
[critical hits](/Rules.aspx?ID=2190), [object immunities](/Rules.aspx?ID=2161), precision damage

</column>

**Dart Volley** <actions string="Reaction" /> **Trigger** A creature enters the hallway or ends its turn in the hallway; **Effect** The trap makes a poisoned dart [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) against the triggering creature, then rolls initiative.

---
**Routine**
(1 action) The trap launches one dart against every creature in the gallery as 1 action. Because it launches darts continuously, the trap can also use the Continuous Barrage free action (see below) to launch darts at each creature during that creature’s turn.

**Ranged** poisoned dart +21, **Damage** 3d4 piercing plus flesset poison; no multiple attack penalty 

**Continuous Barrage** <actions string="Free Action" /> **Trigger** A creature within the active gallery finishes an action; **Effect** The trap makes a poisoned dart Strike against the triggering creature.

**Flesset Poison** (poison) **Saving Throw** DC 22 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 6 rounds; **Stage 1** 1d6 poison damage and [clumsy 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) (1 round); **Stage 2** 2d6 poison damage and clumsy 2 (1 round); **Stage 3** 3d6 poison damage and clumsy 3 (1 round)

---
**Reset**
The trap deactivates and resets after 1 minute.
name: Poisoned Dart Gallery

<title level="1" right="Hazard 3" pfs="">[Quicksand](/Hazards.aspx?ID=493)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Complex" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=30" />
<trait label="Environmental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=62" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 107

**Complexity** Complex

</column>

A 15-foot-wide patch of water and sand attempts to submerge creatures that step onto it.

---

**Stealth**
+12 (trained) (or –10 and no minimum proficiency if the surface is disturbed)

**Disable**
DC 18 [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) (trained) to disturb the surface

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

**Submerge** <actions string="Free Action" /> **Trigger** A Huge or smaller creature walks onto the quicksand; **Effect** The triggering creature sinks into the quicksand up to its waist. The quicksand rolls initiative if it hasn’t already.

---
**Routine**
(1 action) On its initiative, the quicksand pulls down each creature within it. A creature that was submerged up to its waist becomes submerged up to its neck, and a creature that was submerged up to its neck is pulled under and has to hold its breath to avoid [suffocation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2439).

A creature in the quicksand can attempt a DC 20 [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) check to [Swim](/Actions.aspx?ID=2381) to either raise itself by one step if it’s submerged to its neck or worse, or to move 5 feet if it’s submerged only up to its waist. On a critical failure, the creature is pulled down one step. A creature that Swims out of the quicksand escapes the hazard and is prone in a space adjacent to the quicksand patch. Other creatures can [Aid](/Actions.aspx?ID=2292) the creature, typically by using a rope or similar aid, or attempt to pull the creature out with their own DC 20 Athletics check, with the same results as if the creature attempted the check.

---
**Reset**
The hazard still submerges anyone who walks in, but the surface doesn’t become [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=79) again until it settles over the course of 24 hours.
name: Quicksand

<title level="1" right="Hazard 4" pfs="">[Spinning Blade Pillar](/Hazards.aspx?ID=494)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Complex" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=30" />
<trait label="Mechanical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=105" />
<trait label="Trap" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=158" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 107

**Complexity** Complex

</column>

A metal pole with three razor-sharp spinning blades is hidden in the floor, connected to trigger plates in up to eight floor tiles and a hidden control panel within 30 feet.

---

**Stealth**
+11 (trained) or DC 26 (expert) to notice the control panel

**Disable**
DC 21 [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) (trained) twice on the pillar, or DC 19 Thievery (expert) once on the control panel deactivates the whole trap. Breaking the control panel prevents anyone from disabling the trap using the control panel and prevents the trap from deactivating automatically (see Reset below).

<column gap="tiny">

**AC** 21, **Fort** +10, **Ref** +12

**Pillar Hardness**
12

**Pillar HP**
48 (BT 24); **Panel Hardness** 5; **Panel HP** 20 (BT 10)

**Immunities**
[critical hits](/Rules.aspx?ID=2190), [object immunities](/Rules.aspx?ID=2161), precision damage

</column>

**Rising Pillar** <actions string="Reaction" /> **Trigger** A creature steps on one of the trapped floor tiles; **Effect** The trap pops up in a grid intersection and makes a spinning blade [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) against one adjacent creature (if any), then rolls initiative.

---
**Routine**
(3 actions) The trap uses its first action to make a spinning blade Strike against each adjacent creature, its second action to move straight in a random direction (roll 1d4 to determine the direction), and its third to make a spinning blade Strike against each adjacent creature. This trap doesn’t take a multiple attack penalty.

**Speed** 10 feet 

**Melee** <actions string="Single Action" /> spinning blade +12, **Damage** 2d10+5 slashing

---
**Reset**
The trap deactivates and resets after 1 minute.
name: Spinning Blade Pillar

<title level="1" right="Hazard 1" pfs="">[Summoning Rune](/Hazards.aspx?ID=495)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Complex" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=30" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Trap" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=158" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 108, [Pathfinder #181: Zombie Feast](/Sources.aspx?ID=139) pg. 40, [Pathfinder #157: Devil at the Dreaming Palace](/Sources.aspx?ID=45) pg. 51, [Pathfinder #157: Devil at the Dreaming Palace](/Sources.aspx?ID=45) pg. 50

**Complexity** Complex

</column>

A cloud of invisible magical sensors in a 10-foot radius surrounds an invisible wall or floor rune the size of the creature to be summoned.

---

**Stealth**
+7 (trained)

**Disable**
DC 15 [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) to approach without triggering the trap followed by DC 17 [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) (trained) to erase the rune, or [_dispel magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1493) (1st rank; [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=3280) DC 15) to counteract the rune

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

**Summon Monster** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534)) **Trigger** A creature enters the cloud of magical sensors; **Effect** This trap summons a specific 1st-level creature, determined when the trap is created. The creature rolls initiative and remains for 2d6 rounds, after which the spell ends and the creature disappears. The creature also disappears if someone disables the trap before the duration expires. The summoned creature can use 3 actions each round and can use reactions, unlike most summoned creatures.

---
**Reset**
The trap resets each day at dawn.
name: Summoning Rune

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 108 **Complexity** Complex An ornate wheel set into a wall—divided into six segments with colored runes on each—is controlled by a magical sensor that detects any creature within 100 feet in front of it. --- **Stealth** +16 (expert) to detect the magical sensor; noticing the wheel has a DC of 0 **Disable** DC 26 [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) (expert) on the wheel to stop it from spinning, DC 22 Thievery (master) to erase each rune, or [_dispel magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1493) (4th rank; [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=3280) DC 22) to counteract each rune **AC** 24, **Fort** +15, **Ref** +13 **Hardness** 14 **HP** 56 (BT 28) **Immunities** [critical hits](/Rules.aspx?ID=2190), [object immunities](/Rules.aspx?ID=2161), precision damage **Wheel Spin** **Trigger** A creature enters the sensor’s detection area; **Effect** The wheel begins to spin and rolls initiative. --- **Routine** (2 actions) On its initiative, the trap uses its first action to spin, then stops. Roll 1d6 to determine which segment is topmost when the wheel stops spinning. The wheel uses its second action to replicate the spell listed for that segment (3rd rank, DC 24, spell attack modifier +14). This spell’s target is centered on or otherwise includes the nearest creature in the area. This increases the spell’s range to 100 feet if necessary. Any spell cast by this trap is [arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1).
**1** [_sleep_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1675)
**2** [_paralyze_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1622)
**3** [_lightning bolt_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1586) (100-foot line)
**4** [_blindness_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1453)
**5** [_caustic blast_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1461)
**6** [_enfeeble_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1513) --- **Reset** The trap deactivates and resets if 1 minute passes without any creature moving within range of its sensor.
name: Wheel of Misery

<title level="1" right="Hazard 5" pfs="">[Bee-Induced Panic](/Hazards.aspx?ID=497)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=723" />
<trait label="Complex" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=30" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #201: Pactbreaker](/Sources.aspx?ID=241) pg. 15

**Complexity** Complex

</column>

Enraged bees spread panic, causing the crowd to trample each other in a desperate attempt to escape.

---

**Disable**
DC 22 [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) (expert) to calm the panicked crowd and guide them to safety, DC 20 [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=42) (trained) to attend to victims' injuries, or DC 21 [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) (expert) to instruct the crowd on how best to protect themselves from bees. Alternatively, a PC able to communicate with insects can instead attempt a DC 18 Diplomacy (trained) check to calm and redirect the bees. Three successful checks are required to completely disable the hazard. The hazard is automatically disabled after 5 rounds or once both swarms are defeated.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

---
**Routine**
(3 actions) Panicked attendees suffer stings and accidentally injure one another in their fear and confusion. Increase the Casualties score by 1. This increases to 1d3 if one swarm is stinging the crowd, and it increases to 1d3+1 if both swarms are stinging the crowd.
name: Bee-Induced Panic

**Source** [Pathfinder #201: Pactbreaker](/Sources.aspx?ID=241) pg. 42 **Complexity** Complex The illusion shatters, revealing the grove to be a lifeless scar and the seemingly peaceful loggers to be crawling aimlessly about the clearing, consumed by rage and lashing out at anything that comes near. --- **Stealth** +13 (expert) to recognize that the scene before them is an illusion. **Disable** DC 22 [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) (trained) to tame the primal energy, DC 23 [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) (trained) to console the dryad, DC 24 [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) (trained) to identify and heal the emotional wounds; three successes are required to disable the haunt. **AC** 23, **Fort** +11, **Ref** +15, **Will** +15 **Hardness** 12 **HP** 50 (BT 25) **Immunities** critical hits, object immunities, precision damage **Weaknesses** [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509) 8 **Awaken** ([emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) **Trigger** At least two creatures enter the grove; **Effect** The illusion fades, and a sorrowful chill emanates from the giant tree. The haunt rolls initiative. --- **Routine** (1 action; [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) Myristica bemoans her grove's fate, overwhelming foes with sorrow. Each creature in the grove must attempt a DC 23 Will save. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Failure** The creature is [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65) for 1 round and takes 2d6 mental damage.
**Critical Failure** The creature takes 2d6 mental damage, plus 2d6 [persistent mental damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29). While taking this persistent damage, they are [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65). --- **Reset** Unless Silene is destroyed, this haunt can recharge itself after 1 hour.
name: Myristica's Despair

<title level="1" right="Hazard 8" pfs="">[Unwelcome Guests](/Hazards.aspx?ID=502)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Mechanical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=646" />
<trait label="Trap" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=715" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #202: Severed at the Root](/Sources.aspx?ID=242) pg. 20

**Complexity** Simple

</column>

Unwelcome guests who touch this hatch are cursed.

---

**Stealth**
DC 28 (expert)

**Disable**
DC 28 [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) (expert) to drain the magic from the curse, DC 31 [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) (master) to unweave the  curse's bindings, or [_dispel magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1493) (4th rank, [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) DC 28) to counteract the curse

<column gap="tiny">

**AC** 27, **Fort** +19, **Ref** +13

</column>

**Curse Trespassers** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=566), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) **Trigger** A creature touches the hatch; **Effect** The triggering creature takes 3d10+22 force damage and is [knocked back](/Rules.aspx?ID=451) 10 feet. A creature that succeeds at a DC 30 Will save takes half damage and isn't moved. On a failure, the creature is additionally cursed. A cursed creature takes a –2 penalty to Will saves, and whenever the cursed creature becomes affected by a spell, the creature takes 1d10 force damage. The curse remains until removed by [_cleanse affliction_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1467) or similar magic.

---
**Reset**
The trap resets when the hatch is shut.
name: Unwelcome Guests

<title level="1" right="Hazard 6" pfs="">[Spitting Daffodils](/Hazards.aspx?ID=503)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=683" />
<trait label="Environmental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=591" />
<trait label="Plant" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=668" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #202: Severed at the Root](/Sources.aspx?ID=242) pg. 25

**Complexity** Simple

</column>

Daffodils spit acidic nectar at non-plant creatures in the area.

---

**Stealth**
DC 21 (expert)

**Disable**
DC 25 [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) (expert) or DC 25 [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) (trained) to remove the plant's motion sensing filaments

<column gap="tiny">

**AC** 24, **Fort** +11, **Ref** +17

**HP**
54 (BT 27)

**Immunities**
critical hits, object immunities, precision damage

**Weaknesses**
[fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) 5

</column>

**Spit Necter** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=524), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) **Trigger** A non-[plant](/Traits.aspx?ID=668) creature comes within 10 feet; **Effect** The daffodils spit nectar at all non-plant creatures within 10 feet, dealing 4d8+18 acid damage (DC 27 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save).

---
**Reset**
The daffodils replenish their nectar and filaments after 24 hours.
name: Spitting Daffodils

**Source** [Pathfinder #202: Severed at the Root](/Sources.aspx?ID=242) pg. 26 **Complexity** Simple Flowers carved into the door emit colorful pollen when the door is touched. --- **Stealth** DC 28 (expert) **Disable** DC 26 [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) (expert) to deface the flower carvings, DC 25 [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) (expert) to neutralize the pollen, or [_dispel magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1493) (4th rank; [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) DC 26) to counteract the conjuration **AC** 27, **Fort** +19, **Ref** +13 **Hardness** 15 **HP** 62 (BT 31) **Immunities** critical hits, object immunities, precision damage **Pollinate** ([mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)); **Trigger** The door is touched, or the lock is opened; **Effect** The flowers spray poisonous pollen in a 30-foot cone. A creature in the area must attempt a DC 24 Fortitude save, with the following effect. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature takes 2d10+11 poison damage and becomes [enfeebled 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71).
**Failure** The creature takes 3d10+16 poison damage and 1d10 [persistent poison damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29), becomes enfeebled 2, and takes a –2 status penalty to Perception checks for 1d4 hours.
**Critical Failure** The creature takes 4d10+22 poison damage and 1d10 persistent poison damage, becomes enfeebled 4, and takes a –2 status penalty to Perception checks and saves against poison for 24 hours. --- **Reset** The trap resets after 1 hour.
name: Poisonous Pollen Trap

Animated door slams into trespassers.

critical hits, object immunities, precision damage

---
**Reset**
The trap resets automatically.

**Source** [Pathfinder #202: Severed at the Root](/Sources.aspx?ID=242) pg. 33 **Complexity** Complex Animated cabinet imprisons intruders. The cabinet has two compartments; each can hold a single Medium creature or two Small-sized creatures or smaller. --- **Stealth** +20 (expert) **Disable** DC 30 [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) (expert) twice on cabinet; DC 28 [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) or Thievery (expert) to remove each or two doors **AC** 28, **Fort** +21, **Ref** +15 **Cabinet Hardness** 16 **Cabinet HP** 70 (BT 35); **Door Hardness** 10; **Door HP** 40 (BT 20) **Immunities** critical hits, object immunities, precision damage **Its Alive!** ([attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=540)) **Trigger** A living creature moves adjacent to the cabinet; **Effect** The cabinet animates and makes a door Strike against one adjacent creature. The hazard then rolls initiative. --- **Routine** (3 actions) The trap uses its first action to move directly toward the nearest living creature, its second action to make a door Strike against each adjacent creature, and its third action to Batter. This trap doesn't take a multiple attack penalty.
**Speed** 15 feet
**Melee** door +21, **Damage** 2d10+13 bludgeoning plus Capture
**Capture** The target is trapped inside the cabinet's interior, gaining the [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) condition until it [Escapes](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296) (DC 32), another creature [Forces Open](/Actions.aspx?ID=2375) the cabinet door (DC 32), or that cabinet door is destroyed. Each of the two cabinet interiors can contain one Medium creature, or two Small-sized creatures or smaller.
**Batter** ([attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=540)) The cabinet shakes, dealing 2d10+13 bludgeoning damage to all creatures it has [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77). --- **Reset** The trap resets after 1 hour.
name: Consuming Cabinet

**Source** [Pathfinder #202: Severed at the Root](/Sources.aspx?ID=242) pg. 34 **Complexity** Complex Animated awls, hammers, nails, and other tools hurl themselves off the tool bench at intruders. --- **Stealth** +19 (expert) **Disable** DC 33 [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) (master) to soothe the psychic echoes, DC 32 [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) (expert) to overwhelm the area's energy, or DC 32 [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) (expert) to counter the psychic echoes; on the third successful check, the psychic echoes are disrupted, and the trap is destroyed. **Tool Volley** ([attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=540)) **Trigger** A living creature comes within 15 feet of the trap; Effect The trap makes a tool Strike against the triggering creature. The trap then rolls initiative. --- **Routine** (1 action) The trap launches one tool against every living creature within 15 feet as 1 action. Because it flings tools continuously, the trap can also use the Continuous Barrage free action to fling tools at each creature during that creature's turn.
**Ranged** tool +21, **Damage** 2d10+13 bludgeoning; no multiple attack penalty
**Continuous Barrage** **Trigger** A creature within 15 feet finishes an action; **Effect** The trap makes a tool Strike against the triggering creature. --- **Reset** The trap resets after 1 hour.
name: Flinging Tools

<title level="1" right="Hazard 10" pfs="">[Corazal Aflame](/Hazards.aspx?ID=508)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Complex" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=30" />
<trait label="Environmental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=591" />
<trait label="Fire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=604" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #202: Severed at the Root](/Sources.aspx?ID=242) pg. 35

**Complexity** Complex

</column>

Fire burns Corazal and threatens to spread to the surrounding forest.

---

**Stealth**
–10

**Disable**
DC 30 [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) or [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) (expert) to douse flames or DC 32 [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) (expert) to smother flames; on the sixth successful check, the flames go out. Casting a non-[cantrip](/Traits.aspx?ID=550) [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=732) spell on the flames counts as one success.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

**Blaze** <actions string="Reaction" /> **Trigger** A living creature approaches Corazal; **Effect** The flames blaze higher, dealing 2d12+13 fire damage to the triggering creature (DC 29 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). The hazard then rolls initiative.

---
**Routine**
(1 action; [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604)) On its turn, the fire deals 2d8+13 fire damage to creatures in the grove, plus an additional 6 fire damage to Corazal and any creature within 15 feet of them (DC 29 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save).
name: Corazal Aflame

**Source** [Pathfinder #202: Severed at the Root](/Sources.aspx?ID=242) pg. 46 **Complexity** Complex Three shadow tendrils emerge from the ground and seize trespassers. --- **Stealth** +15 (expert) **Disable** DC 32 [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) or [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) (expert) to banish one of the three the shadow tendrils or DC 34 [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) (master) twice to confound the bowl's magical sensors; **Bypass** A creature who places an offering worth at least 10 gp into the bowl doesn't trigger the trap and isn't targeted by the trap. **AC** 27, **Fort** +16, **Ref** +22 **Shadow Tendril Hardness** 15 **Shadow Tendril HP** 30 (BT 15) **Immunities** critical hits, object immunities, precision damage **Shadow Tendrils** **Trigger** A living creature passes by the statue without placing an offering worth at least 10 gp in the bowl; **Effect** Shadow tendrils rise from the ground and make a lash Strike against the triggering creature. The trap then rolls initiative --- **Routine** (2 actions per shadow tendril) On its turn, each shadow tendril uses 1 action to make a lash Strike against a creature in the area, then uses 1 action to Constrict a creature it has [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77). The shadow tendrils don't apply the trap's multiple attack penalty.
**Melee** lash +23 ([reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d12+13 bludgeoning plus [Improved Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=66) ([Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296) DC 33)
[**Constrict**](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=57) 2d12 bludgeoning, DC 33 --- **Reset** The trap resets after 10 minutes.
name: Punish the Impertinent

<title level="1" right="Hazard 10" pfs="">[Shadow Roil](/Hazards.aspx?ID=510)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=683" />
<trait label="Complex" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=30" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
<trait label="Shadow" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=693" />
<trait label="Trap" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=715" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #202: Severed at the Root](/Sources.aspx?ID=242) pg. 53

**Complexity** Complex

</column>

A cyclone of living shadows and planar magic surges around the area, attracted to living creatures.

---

**Stealth**
+15 (expert)

**Disable**
DC 32 [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) or [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) (expert) to weaken the tornado; three successes in any combination are required to disable the trap.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

**Touch Down** <actions string="Reaction" /> **Trigger** Two or more living creatures enter the area; **Effect** A spiraling column of shadows and crackling planar magic gathers 20 feet away, becoming a whirling cyclone of darkness 15 feet wide and 100 feet tall. The trap then rolls initiative.

---
**Routine**
(3 actions) The cyclone uses 3 actions to move, traveling up to 30 feet with each action and dealing 2d12+13 slashing damage to each creature in its path (DC 33 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). A creature needs to attempt only one save during the cyclone's movement, even if the cyclone moves over its space more than once. On a critical failure, a creature is swept up into the cyclone, becoming [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) ([Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296) DC 33). A creature grabbed by the cyclone moves along with the cyclone and takes 1d12+7 additional slashing damage at the beginning of its turn, and it must attempt the [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save against the cyclone on the cyclone's turn, no matter where the cyclone moves. A creature that successfully Escapes from the cyclone [falls](/Rules.aspx?ID=402) from a height of 1d20 × 5 feet.

---
**Reset**
The cyclone disperses after 1 minute and resets after 1 day.
name: Shadow Roil

<title level="1" right="Hazard 9" pfs="">[Puncturing Spire](/Hazards.aspx?ID=511)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
<trait label="Trap" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=715" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #202: Severed at the Root](/Sources.aspx?ID=242) pg. 55

**Complexity** Simple

</column>

A metal spire bursts from the ground, skewering the target and knocking aside other creatures.

---

**Stealth**
DC 23 (trained)

**Disable**
DC 30 [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) or [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) (expert) to seal the planar breach, or DC 28 [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) (master) to magically reinforce the ground and prevent a spire's emergence

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

**Pierce Realm** <actions string="Reaction" /> **Trigger** A living creature enters the area; **Effect** A 60-foot-tall metal spire rises from the ground, puncturing the triggering creature and dealing 4d10+26 piercing damage (DC 32 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). On a critical failure, the creature is additionally impaled, becoming [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) until it [Escapes](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296) (DC 30). A creature that Escapes is 60 feet in the air. Additionally, all creatures within 10- feet of the spire take 2d10+13 bludgeoning damage (DC 32 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). On a failure, they're knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88).
name: Puncturing Spire

markdown:
**Source** [Pathfinder #203 Shepherd of Decay](/Sources.aspx?ID=243) pg. 18 **Complexity** Complex Countless fey with grisly visages and misshapen bodies cavort throughout the room, kicking up caustic debris and pulling other creatures into their dangerous dance. --- **Stealth** +20 (trained) **Disable** DC 32 [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) or [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) (expert) to perform a ritual to ward the area against fey intrusion, DC 34 [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) (master) to meet the Twisted King's demands with a disturbing dance, or DC 32 [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) or [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) (expert) to fend off or frighten the fey dancers. Three total successes are required to disable the haunt, and each check takes 2 actions. **Welcome to the Feywrithe** **Trigger** 1 round passes since both isqulugs in area **B6** are slain, or 10 rounds pass since they rolled initiative; **Effect** The isqulugs die horribly (if they're still alive), rent by clawed fey hands. Spectral fey pull at the other creatures in the caves, all of which must attempt a DC 36 Fortitude save. The haunt then rolls initiative. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is [pulled](/Rules.aspx?ID=451) 10 feet toward the standing stones.
**Failure** The creature is pulled 15 feet toward the standing stones.
**Critical Failure** The creature is pulled 15 feet toward the standing stones, knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88), and takes 1d6 bludgeoning damage (in addition to any damage from the toxic water). --- **Routine** (1 action) Spectral fey cavort about the room. All creatures in the room are raked by ghostly claws and splashed with toxic water, taking 1d10+14 force damage and 2d10 acid damage (DC 32 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). Creatures that critically fail their Reflex saves are [pulled](/Rules.aspx?ID=451) 10 feet toward the standing stones. --- **Reset** The haunt deactivates after 1 minute. It re-forms every new moon. If all of the glyphs in area **B6** are restored—a process requiring 10 days of downtime and 10 successful DC 36 [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) or [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) checks—the haunt becomes permanently disabled and doesn't re-form.
name: Twisted King's Court

**Source** [Pathfinder #203 Shepherd of Decay](/Sources.aspx?ID=243) pg. 21 **Complexity** Complex A mass of petrified lichen takes the shape of a beast and lurches forward. --- **Stealth** +26 (expert) **Disable** DC 33 [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) or [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) (master) to scrape away lichen without being exposed, or DC 36 [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=42) or [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) (expert) to concoct a natural remedy and weaken the hazard; four total successes are required to disable it **AC** 31, **Fort** +26, **Ref** +24 **Hardness** 20 **HP** 80 (BT 40) **Immunities** critical hits, object immunities, precision damage **Weaknesses** [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) 10 **Lurching Lichen** **Trigger** Three or more creatures not infected with [leechlichen](/Diseases.aspx?ID=40) enter the area of the hazard; Effect The mass of lichen lurches forward, taking up one 5-foot square and forming a beast-like shape. The hazard rolls initiative. --- **Routine** (3 actions) The lichen monstrosity uses 3 actions to move, traveling up to 30 feet with each action and dealing 2d10+10 bludgeoning damage. Each creature in its path must attempt a DC 31 Reflex save. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature takes half damage.
**Failure** The creature takes full damage and is exposed to leechlichen.
**Critical Failure** The creature takes double damage and is exposed to leechlichen. The results of its saving throw against the leechlichen are treated as one step worse. Each successful check to disable this hazard reduces its movement by 30 feet, and the fourth success disables it completely. --- **Reset** The trap resets over the course of an hour.
name: Lichen Monstrosity

markdown:
**Source** [Claws of the Tyrant](/Sources.aspx?ID=266) pg. 28 **Complexity** Complex The spirits of community members linger on after their tragic deaths. --- **Stealth** +10 (trained) **Disable** DC 20 [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) (untrained) to [Request](/Actions.aspx?ID=2393) that the spirits leave or DC 18 [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) (trained) to exorcise the spirits **Manifest** ([auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) **Trigger** A creature the spirits knew in life enters the haunted area; **Effect** The spirits manifest with a terrible wailing. Each creature in the area must succeed at a DC 20 Will save or be [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 1 (or frightened 2 on a critical failure). Frightened creatures are [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=81) for as long as they remain frightened. The haunt then rolls initiative. --- **Routine** (1 action; [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) The haunt attempts an [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) check with a +11 modifier against the Will DC of each frightened creature as it bombards them with the voices of their dead loved ones. **Critical Success** The creature takes 2d4 mental damage and its frightened condition increases by 1, to a maximum of frightened 3.
**Success** The creature takes 1d4 mental damage.
**Failure** The creature is unaffected.
**Critical Failure** The creature’s frightened condition is reduced by 1. --- **Reset** The haunt falls silent for 24 hours once all frightened creatures die or reduce their frightened condition’s value to 0. During this time, a creature that isn’t frightened can exorcise the spirits with a DC 18 Religion check, permanently destroying the haunt.
name: Haunting Echoes

**Failure** The creature is unaffected.

**Source** [Claws of the Tyrant](/Sources.aspx?ID=266) pg. 35 **Complexity** Simple The 30-foot-long, 10-foot-wide bridge groans under the weight of those who cross it. --- **Stealth** DC 14 (trained) **Perilous Sway** **Trigger** A Medium or larger creature Strides 10 feet or more in the bridge’s area; **Effect** All creatures on the bridge must attempt a DC 18 Reflex save. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) 1 until the beginning of its next turn.
**Failure** The creature falls [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88).
**Critical Failure** The creature falls from the bridge, landing in the ravine 20 feet below.
**Collapse** Each time the decrepit bridge triggers its Perilous Sway after the first, attempt a DC 5 [flat check](/Rules.aspx?ID=333). On a failure, the bridge collapses. All creatures on the bridge fall 20 feet into the ravine, taking falling damage normally.
name: Decrepit Bridge

<title level="1" right="Hazard 7" pfs="">[Snake's Mane](/Hazards.aspx?ID=517)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Environmental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=62" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Claws of the Tyrant](/Sources.aspx?ID=266) pg. 48

**Complexity** Simple

</column>

A tangle of feathery green vines appears innocuous but fires a spray of acidic barbs at anything that disturbs it.

---

**Stealth**
DC 27 (trained) to discern the snake’s mane from the surrounding vegetation

**Disable**
Disable DC 27 [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) (expert) to strip the barbs from the vines, rendering them harmless, or DC 30 [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) (trained) to move through the snake’s mane without disturbing it

<column gap="tiny">

**AC** 22, **Fort** +12, **Ref** +0

**Hardness**
15

**Immunities**
critical hits, [object immunities](/Rules.aspx?ID=2161), precision damage

**Weaknesses**
fire 10, slashing 10

</column>

**Barb Spray** <actions string="Reaction" />  **Trigger** A creature enters an area containing snake’s mane or the snake’s mane takes damage; **Effect** The vine sprays a hail of acidic barbs in a 10-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387). Any creature in the area takes 4d10+18 acid damage. On a critical failure, the creature is also [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=59) for 1 minute.

---
**Reset**
1 hour, as the vine grows a new set of barbs
name: Snake's Mane

<title level="1" right="Hazard 7" pfs="">[Chernasardo Chompers](/Hazards.aspx?ID=518)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Mechanical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=105" />
<trait label="Snare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=146" />
<trait label="Trap" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=158" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Claws of the Tyrant](/Sources.aspx?ID=266) pg. 48

**Complexity** Simple

</column>

These animal snares have been repurposed to discourage unwanted intruders. When a creature steps onto the snare, jaws with sharpened teeth close around the creature’s leg.

---

**Stealth**
DC 27 (trained) to spot the snare

**Disable**
DC 26 [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) (trained) to step clear the snare before it springs, or DC 22 [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) (trained) to trigger the trap harmlessly

<column gap="tiny">

**AC** 24, **Fort** +14, **Ref** +0

**Hardness**
15

**HP**
60 (BT 30)

**Immunities**
critical hits, [object immunities](/Rules.aspx?ID=2161), precision damage

</column>

**Bite** <actions string="Reaction" /> **Trigger** A creature moves into the space containing the trap; **Effect** The trap’s jaws snap shut on the creature’s leg, dealing 4d8+14 piercing damage (DC 26 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). On a failure, the creature becomes caught in the trap and is [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=81) ([Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296) DC 22).
name: Chernasardo Chompers

<title level="1" right="Hazard 7" pfs="">[Electrified Entrance](/Hazards.aspx?ID=521)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Trap" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=158" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Claws of the Tyrant](/Sources.aspx?ID=266) pg. 49

**Complexity** Simple

</column>

Magical runes electrocute anyone who crosses the door’s threshold.

---

**Stealth**
DC 24 (trained) to spot the runes etched into the door’s frame

**Disable**
DC 26 [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) (trained) to jump through the door before the runes trigger, DC 28 [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) (expert) to scratch out the runes, or [_dispel magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1493) (4th rank; [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) DC 24) to counteract the runes

<column gap="tiny">

**AC** 22, **Fort** +18, **Ref** +12

**Hardness**
14

**HP**
58 (BT 26)

**Immunities**
critical hits, [object immunities](/Rules.aspx?ID=2161), precision damage

</column>

**Sizzle** <actions string="Reaction" /> **Trigger** A creature moves through the doorway; **Effect** Arcs of lightning flash across the threshold. The triggering creature takes 4d8+18 electricity damage (DC 23 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). On a failure, the creature is also [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65) for 1 minute. On a critical failure, the creature is [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=59) for 1 minute and then dazzled for an additional minute.

---
**Reset**
1 minute
name: Electrified Entrance

**Source** [Claws of the Tyrant](/Sources.aspx?ID=266) pg. 49 **Complexity** Simple A rune inscribed on the inside of the lock releases a burst of flame that burns the thief’s hands and their tools. --- **Stealth** DC 27 (trained) to spot the rune inside the lock **Disable** DC 30 [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) (expert) to scratch out the rune, or [_dispel magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1493) (4th rank; [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) DC 24) to counteract the runes **AC** 22, **Fort** +13, **Ref** +17 **Hardness** 14 **HP** 58 (BT 36) **Immunities** critical hits, [object immunities](/Rules.aspx?ID=2161), precision damage **Flash Flame** **Trigger** A creature attempts to Pick the Lock or fails to [Disable the Device](/Actions.aspx?ID=2411); **Effect** The rune triggers, releasing a burst of flame from the lock. The triggering creature must attempt a DC 25 Reflex saving throw. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature takes 2d10+9 fire damage.
**Failure** The creature takes 4d10+18 fire damage and their fingers are burned, imposing a –1 circumstance penalty to [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) checks for 1 hour.
**Critical Failure** As failure, and the creature’s [thieves’ toolkit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2758) is [broken](/Conditions.aspx?ID=60).
name: Thieves' Lament

**Source** [Claws of the Tyrant](/Sources.aspx?ID=266) pg. 51 **Complexity** Simple A magical flame changes color in response to Fort Ozem’s alert status, with a 10-foot radius. --- **Stealth** DC 22 (expert) **Disable** DC 30 [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43), [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44), or [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) (expert) to snuff out the flame with opposing magic; each success reduces the flame’s color one step (from red to blue, blue to yellow, and so on). A critical success reduces the flame’s color two steps. A success while the flame is white permanently disables the hazard. **Flaring Flame** **Trigger** A creature moves into the radius of the flame’s light or starts its turn in the radius; **Effect** [Iomedae’s](/Deities.aspx?ID=285) Flame flares, producing an effect determined by the flame’s current color. Followers of Iomedae treat the result of all saving throws against the flame’s effects as one step better. The effects are cumulative; a creature that enters the radius of the red flame also triggers the effect of the white, yellow, and blue flames.
**White Flame** The flame casts [_revealing light_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1653) (DC 28).
**Yellow Flame** The flame casts [_fear_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1524) (DC 28) on each creature in the light’s radius.
**Blue Flame** The flame flickers as winds buffet creatures in the area. Any creature in the flame’s light takes 8d4 bludgeoning damage (DC 28 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). This damage has the [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661) trait against living creatures.
**Red Flame** The flame releases a sudden pulse of intense heat. Any creature in the flame’s light takes 4d6 fire damage and 4d6 spirit damage (DC 28 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Fortitude save). This damage has the [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517) trait. --- **Reset** The hazard resets automatically each round. After 1 hour, the flame reverts to its default status of a white flame.
name: Iomedae's Flame

<title level="1" right="Hazard 8" pfs="">[Names of the Nameless](/Hazards.aspx?ID=524)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Complex" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=30" />
<trait label="Haunt" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=88" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Claws of the Tyrant](/Sources.aspx?ID=266) pg. 57

**Complexity** Complex

</column>

The voices of the nameless dead intrude into the minds of the living.

---

**Stealth**
DC +16 (trained) to hear whispering voices

**Disable**
DC 26 Perception to discern a name spoken by the nameless dead, or DC 28 [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) (expert) to put the nameless dead to rest. Speaking three names aloud disables the haunt. A creature that speaks any other name aloud must succeed at a DC 28 Will save or be [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=63) for 1 round.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

**Disappering Door** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=629), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) **Trigger** A creature enters the room; **Effect** An illusory wall appears across the room’s entrance. A creature who succeeds at DC 30 Perception check disbelieves the illusion, allowing them to leave the area. The haunt then rolls initiative.

---
**Routine**
(1 action; [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) Spirits of the dead whisper their names into the minds of creatures in the room, dealing 2d10+11 mental damage (DC 28 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Will save). A creature that fails the save is also [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 1 (or frightened 2 on a critical failure).

---
**Reset**
If the haunt is disabled, divine light washes through the area. Living creatures in the area regain 4d8 Hit Points, and undead creatures in the area take 4d8 [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509) damage. The haunt deactivates 1 minute after all creatures leave the area but resets immediately thereafter.
name: Names of the Nameless
