<title level="1" right="Hazard 16" pfs="">[Collapsing Bridge](/Hazards.aspx?ID=525)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="Complex" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=30" />
<trait label="Mechanical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=105" />
<trait label="Trap" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=158" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Claws of the Tyrant](/Sources.aspx?ID=266) pg. 67, [Kingmaker Companion Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=151) pg. 31, [Pathfinder #184: The Ghouls Hunger](/Sources.aspx?ID=147) pg. 63

**Complexity** Complex

</column>

Metal supports twist and shear off the bridge, causing stretches of the structure to collapse.

---

**Stealth**
+38 (expert)

**Disable**
DC 40 [Engineering Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) to identify and reinforce weak points, DC 43 [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) (master) to construct secondary supports, or DC 45 [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) (legendary) to reinforce the bridge with found materials; three successes are required to halt the collapse

<column gap="tiny">

**AC** 39, **Fort** +30, **Ref** +25

**Hardness**
25

**HP**
104 (BT 52)

**Immunities**
critical hits, [object immunities](/Rules.aspx?ID=2161), precision damage

</column>

**Bridge Collapse** <actions string="Reaction" /> **Trigger** A Medium or larger creature steps onto the bridge’s weak point; **Effect** The bridge collapses in the creature’s square, then rolls initiative. The DC to [Grab an Edge](/Actions.aspx?ID=2313) on the bridge is 38. Once the bridge begins to collapse, the river’s Swim DC increases to 36 as the water churns with the falling debris. A creature that starts its turn swimming within 15 feet of the bridge takes 10d6 bludgeoning damage (DC 41 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save).

---
**Routine**
(1 action) The collapse radiates out from the damaged section of the bridge. All squares adjacent to a collapsed section also fall into the water.
name: Collapsing Bridge

<title level="1" right="Hazard 18" pfs="">[Hardened Entry](/Hazards.aspx?ID=526)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Complex" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=30" />
<trait label="Mechanical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=105" />
<trait label="Trap" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=158" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Claws of the Tyrant](/Sources.aspx?ID=266) pg. 68

**Complexity** Complex

</column>

A portcullis drops from the ceiling, landing with a heavy thunk. Seconds later, a cloud of acid rises from a metal grate in the floor.

---

**Stealth**
+35 (master)

**Disable**
DC 45 [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) to prevent the portcullis from dropping, DC 48 [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) (master) to create a compound to neutralize the cloud of acid, or DC 35 [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) (expert) to jam the pressure plate

<column gap="tiny">

**AC** 42, **Fort** +33, **Ref** +27

**Hardness**
29

**HP**
110 (BT 55)

**Immunities**
critical hits, [object immunities](/Rules.aspx?ID=2161), precision damage

</column>

**Falling Portcullis** <actions string="Reaction" /> **Trigger** A creature Strides beneath the portcullis while it is raised; **Effect** The portcullis slams down, driving its sharpened ends through a grate in the floor. Creatures directly beneath the portcullis take 3d12+20 piercing damage (DC 40 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). The portcullis is made of [adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2915) and has Hardness 31 and 120 HP (BT 60). The trap then rolls initiative.

---
**Routine**
(1 action) A cloud of acidic vapor rises from the grate beneath the portcullis in a 5-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387). Any creature that enters the area or starts its turn inside the cloud takes 3d12+20 acid damage (DC 40 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Fortitude save). A creature that critically fails the save also takes 1d12 [persistent acid damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86). Each time the hazard uses this routine beyond the first, increase the radius of the emanation by 5 feet.

---
**Reset**
Once triggered, the trap must be manually reset by raising the portcullis. Once all the vials are smashed, the trap can’t use its routine again until the vials are replaced.
name: Hardened Entry

**Source** [Claws of the Tyrant](/Sources.aspx?ID=266) pg. 68 **Complexity** Simple Spores explode onto nearby creatures, leaving welts as the mold dissolves exposed flesh. --- **Stealth** DC 36 (expert) **Disable** DC 42 [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) (legendary) to neutralize the void energy that animates the mold, or DC 36 [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) (master) to remove the mold without triggering the spores **AC** 40, **Fort** +37, **Ref** +29 **HP** 130 (BT 65) **Immunities** critical hits, [object immunities](/Rules.aspx?ID=2161), precision damage **Weaknesses** [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509) 20 **Spore Explosion** **Trigger** A living creature moves adjacent to the mold or damages the mold; **Effect** All creatures within 15 feet of the mold are exposed to the flesh-eating spores.
**Flesh-Eating Spores** ([contact](/Traits.aspx?ID=565), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510)) The flesh-eating spores leave red rings on the skin as they dissolve the target’s flesh; **Saving Throw** DC 44 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 6 rounds; **Stage 1** 4d10 void damage and [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) 1 (1 round); **Stage 2** 6d10 void damage and drained 2 (1 round); **Stage 3** 8d10 void damage and drained 3 (1 round)
name: Carnivorous Mold

The hazard has an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) modifier of +37 for its [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) ([Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296) DC 47).
Melee gauntlet +37 ([magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)), Damage 10d6+35 bludgeoning plus [Improved Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=66) --- **Reset** 1 hour
name: Grasping Gauntlet

**Source** [Claws of the Tyrant](/Sources.aspx?ID=266) pg. 74 **Complexity** Complex Deadbolts inside the doors clunk as three glowing runes appear on the ceiling. --- **Stealth** +37 (master) **Disable** DC 42 [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43), [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44), or [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) to erase the runes, or DC 46 [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) or [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) to destroy the runes, or [_dispel magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1493) (9th rank, [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) DC 41); each rune requires a success to remove (three runes total) **AC** 40, **Fort** +38, **Ref** +29, **Will** +35 **Hardness** 31 **HP** 130 (BT 65) **Deadbolts** **Trigger** A creature approaches within 10 feet of the cleansing suite’s eastern doors; **Effect** The doors to the chamber slam shut and seal with deadbolts. The hazard rolls initiative. --- **Routine** (3 actions) The cleansing suite activates one of its runes. The hazard can’t activate the same rune more than once per turn. Disabling a rune removes one of the hazard’s actions and prevents the hazard from activating that rune.
**Rune Against Evil** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517)) The hazard fills the chamber with holy energy that deals 6d6+20 spirit damage (DC 41 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Fortitude save) to creatures with the [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521) trait.
**Rune Against Magic** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) The hazard casts [_dispel magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1493) (9th rank, [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) +31) that targets a spell or effect on each creature in the chamber.
**Rune Against Scrying** ([detection](/Traits.aspx?ID=574), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) The hazard casts [_detect scrying_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1487). If it detects a [scrying](/Traits.aspx?ID=689) effect in the chamber, it casts [_hidden mind_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1556) on each affected creature and _dispel magic_ on the effect (9th rank, counteract +31). --- **Reset** 1 minute after the hazard activates, the deadbolts unlock and the hazard resets.
name: Cleansing Suite

markdown:
**Source** [Claws of the Tyrant](/Sources.aspx?ID=266) pg. 99 **Complexity** Complex Seldeg’s ritual nears its conclusion, causing magical energy to arc erratically between the ritual circles. --- **Stealth** +25 (master) to notice the buildup of divine energy and anticipate its discharge. **Disable** DC 44 [Arazni Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) or Iomedae Lore to recount a tale from history that grounds that deity in the current reality, DC 50 [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) (legendary) to create a device to siphon energy from the ritual circle, or DC 46 [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) (legendary) to discharge the escalating divine energy; six successes (three successes per circle) are required to disarm the hazard **Reorder Reality** **Trigger** A creature other than [Seldeg](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3832) enters the area contained by the ritual circles; **Effect** Arcs of divine energy travel between the two circles. Each creature within the circles takes 4d10+26 spirit damage (DC 45 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Fortitude save). If one of the ritual circles has been disabled, the hazard instead affects all creature in the room, but each creature gains a +2 circumstance bonus to their save. The hazard rolls initiative. --- **Routine** (1 action; [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) The divine energy gathered by the ritual spikes. One of the eleven stained-glass windows (each depicting one of her Eleven Acts) transforms to feature [Arazni](/Deities.aspx?ID=326) instead. Any creature that can see the window change must attempt a DC 50 Will save.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected and is immune to this effect for 10 minutes.
**Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Failure** The creature takes 3d12+20 mental damage.
**Critical Failure** The creature takes 6d12+40 mental damage. If the creatures’ next d20 roll is a critical success, they earn a critical failure instead. This is a [misfortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=654) effect.
If all 11 windows transform, Seldeg’s ritual finishes ahead of schedule. The stained-glass windows shatter, dealing 8d10+52 piercing damage (DC 50 Reflex save) to each creature in the room. The hazard then becomes inactive. --- **Reset** While atop the floating platform, Seldeg Bhedlis can use a 3-action activity with the [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541) trait to reenergize the ritual. Doing so removes one success to disable the hazard and immediately triggers the hazard’s Reorder Reality reaction.
name: Twist of Fate

<title level="1" right="Hazard 0" pfs="">[Animal Pit](/Hazards.aspx?ID=531)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Mechanical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=105" />
<trait label="Trap" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=158" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #213: Thirst for Blood](/Sources.aspx?ID=257) pg. 23

**Complexity** Simple

</column>

A weight-activated trap door covers a pit that’s 10 feet square and 20 feet deep.

---

**Stealth**
DC 18 (DC 14 if the trap has been activated within the last day, or 0 if the trap door is disabled or [broken](/Conditions.aspx?ID=60))

**Disable**
DC 12 [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) to remove the trap door

<column gap="tiny">

**AC** 10, **Fort** +1, **Ref** +1

**Trapdoor Hardness**
3

**Trapdoor HP**
12 (BT 6)

**Immunities**
[critical hits](/Rules.aspx?ID=2190), [object immunities](/Rules.aspx?ID=2161), precision damage

</column>

**Pitfall** <actions string="Reaction" /> **Trigger** A creature weighing at least 20 pounds walks onto the trap door; **Effect** The triggering creature falls in and takes falling damage (typically 10 bludgeoning damage). That creature can use the [Grab an Edge](/Actions.aspx?ID=2313) reaction to avoid falling.

---
**Reset**
The trapdoors automatically reset to their closed position once a creature falls in, allowing for more creatures to fall in, though any camouflage on the trap door falls in as well, making it easier for creatures to notice the trap door.
name: Animal Pit

<title level="1" right="Hazard 1" pfs="">[Trapped Gate](/Hazards.aspx?ID=532)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Mechanical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=105" />
<trait label="Trap" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=158" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #213: Thirst for Blood](/Sources.aspx?ID=257) pg. 33

**Complexity** Simple

</column>

A hair trigger rings an alarm and launches a small assortment of wooden spikes to impale anyone attempting to open the gate incorrectly.

---

**Stealth**
DC 17 (trained)

**Disable**
DC 15 [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) (trained) to disable the thin trigger that snaps when attempting to open the gate.

<column gap="tiny">

**AC** 16, **Fort** +10, **Ref** +2

**Hardness**
5

**HP**
20 (BT 10)

**Immunities**
[critical hits](/Rules.aspx?ID=2190), [object immunities](/Rules.aspx?ID=2161), precision damage

</column>

**Impale Trespasser** <actions string="Reaction" /> **Trigger** A creature attempts to open the gate; **Effect** A loud alarm rings and set of wooden spikes swings up from below and over the gate, dealing 3d8 piercing damage to the triggering creature (DC 17 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). If the creature takes damage from the spikes, they also become [Grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) by the gate. The creature can attempt to [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296) (DC 17). If the creature succeeded at their basic Reflex save, they gain a +1 circumstance bonus to their checks to Escape, as the spikes didn’t fully impale them. If the creature critically failed their basic Reflex save, they take a –2 circumstance penalty to their checks to Escape, as the spikes are embedded particularly deep.
name: Trapped Gate

<title level="1" right="Hazard 1" pfs="">[Camazotz's Swarm](/Hazards.aspx?ID=533)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Complex" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=30" />
<trait label="Environmental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=62" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #213: Thirst for Blood](/Sources.aspx?ID=257) pg. 43

**Complexity** Complex

</column>

A massive swarm of bats flies throughout the room, hampering vision, attacking foes, and magically restoring [Camazotz’s](/Deities.aspx?ID=334) followers.

---

**Stealth**
+7 (trained)

**Disable**
DC 17 [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) (trained) to disperse the swarm or DC 15 [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) (trained) to disrupt Camazotz’s connection, which disperses the swarm

<column gap="tiny">

**AC** 19, **Fort** +10, **Ref** +11

**HP**
26

**Immunities**
[critical hits](/Rules.aspx?ID=2190), precision damage

</column>

**Swarming Descent** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) **Trigger** A creature that’s not a worshipper of Camazotz enters the room; **Effect** The hundreds of bats descend down into the room in a terrifying, shrieking mass. The swarm occupies a 30-foot [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385) on the floor of the room. All enemies within this area must attempt a DC 17 Will save. On a failure, a creature becomes [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 1 (frightened 2 on a critical failure). The swarm then rolls initiative.

---
**Routine**
(2 actions) The swarm uses its first action to move up to 30 feet to envelop as many enemies as possible within its 30-foot [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385) space. All enemies in the swarm’s space become dazzled while within the swarm. It then uses its second action to bite all enemies, dealing 1d6+3 piercing damage (DC 17 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save) to all enemies in the space. 

**Restore Follower** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) **Requirements** An ally is in the swarm’s space; **Trigger** The swarm dealt damage to an enemy; **Effect** The swarm takes some of the blood it drank from an enemy and feeds it to an ally in its space. The ally restores Hit Points equal to the highest damage the swarm dealt to an enemy this turn.

---
**Reset**
The swarm disperses and returns to roost throughout the room 1 minute after all enemies leave the room. The swarm recovers 5 Hit Points per day thanks the power of Camazotz.
name: Camazotz's Swarm

**Source** [Pathfinder #214: The Broken Palace](/Sources.aspx?ID=258) pg. 10 **Complexity** Simple A section of ceiling and iron cell bars collapse inward. --- **Stealth** DC 22 (expert) **Disable** DC 22 [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) (trained) or DC 25 [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) (trained) to use a loose iron bar to shore up the ceiling. **Collapse** **Trigger** A Medium or larger creature moves through the cell at a faster pace than simply Stepping, or more than one Medium or larger creature enter the cell at the same time; **Effect** The ceiling and walls of the cell collapse, creating a thunderous roar. All creatures in the cell must attempt a DC 25 Reflex save; the cell then becomes greater [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2366) that can be passed through safely.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature takes 1d10+6 bludgeoning damage.
**Failure** The creature takes 2d10+13 bludgeoning damage and is immobilized by fallen rubble ([Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296) DC 25).
**Critical Failure** The creature is buried in the rubble. They take 3d10+19 bludgeoning damage and are [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90) (Escape DC 25). Until they escape, the creature must hold their breath or risk [suffocating](/Rules.aspx?ID=2439).
name: Collapsing Cell

<title level="1" right="Hazard 4" pfs="">[Breakaway Ladder](/Hazards.aspx?ID=535)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Mechanical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=105" />
<trait label="Trap" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=158" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #214: The Broken Palace](/Sources.aspx?ID=258) pg. 15

**Complexity** Simple

</column>

The rope ladder rips free from its anchors and drops into the pit.

---

**Stealth**
DC 22 (expert)

**Disable**
DC 25 [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) (trained) or DC 22 [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) (trained) to secure the ladder’s anchors so they won’t break

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

**Rip Free** <actions string="Reaction" /> **Trigger** More than 3 Small creatures or more than 1 Medium creature climbs the ladder at the same time; **Effect** The ladder’s anchors rip free from the wall, dropping the ladder and anyone on it into area **B1**. Anyone on the ladder falls 50 feet, taking falling damage as appropriate. A creature at the top of the ladder can attempt a DC 21 [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) check or Reflex save to [Grab an Edge](/Actions.aspx?ID=2313) and cling to the side of the pit in area **A9**. A creature lower on the ladder can attempt to grab a handhold on the shaft’s wall, but in this case the check increases to DC 25.
name: Breakaway Ladder

**Source** [Pathfinder #214: The Broken Palace](/Sources.aspx?ID=258) pg. 28 **Complexity** Complex Bodiless screams arise as the room rumbles and shakes, as if in the grip of an earthquake. --- **Stealth** +13 (expert) **Disable** DC 23 [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) (expert) to exorcise the spirits or DC 23 [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) to stand resolute and show the spirits there’s nothing to fear; three total successes are required to disable this haunt. **Final Cries of Horror** ([emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) **Trigger** A living creature attempts to exit the room; **Effect** The cavern erupts into terrified cries as a deep rumbling fills the room. All creatures in area **C12** must succeed at a DC 22 Will save or become [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 1 (frightened 2 on a critical failure). The haunt then rolls initiative. --- **Routine** (1 action; [illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=629), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) A thunderous explosion rocks the room as the ceiling appears to split open and collapse. This cave-in is an illusion formed from the memories of the dead, but it can inflict real damage. All creatures in the room must attempt a DC 22 Will save.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature takes 1d10 mental damage.
**Failure** The creature takes 1d10+6 mental damage and believes the room is filling with rubble—they're [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) 1 until the start of their next turn as they spend their energy navigating the illusory rockfall.
**Critical Failure** The creature takes 2d10+12 mental damage and believes they're pinned by rubble—they're [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=81) until the start of their next turn. --- **Reset** The haunt deactivates once no living creatures are in the cave at the end of a round, then resets 1 hour later as the spiritual energy rebuilds to a breaking point.
name: Echoes of Earthfall

**Source** [Pathfinder #215: To Blot Out the Sun](/Sources.aspx?ID=259) pg. 8 **Complexity** Complex Four magical prisms integrated into an _aeon stone matrix_ fire energy beams at intruders. The trap doesn't target creatures wearing [security badges](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3796), or in the custody of a creature wearing a _security badge_. --- **Stealth** +16 (expert) **Disable** [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) DC 23 (expert) to disconnect one prism from the _aeon stone matrix_, or [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) DC 21 (trained) to disrupt one prism’s magical energy, or [_dispel magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1493) (3rd rank; [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) DC 23) to counteract one prism; four total successes are required to disable the trap. **AC** 22, **Fort** +15, **Ref** +9 **Prism Hardness** 12 **Prism HP** 14 (BT 7) **Immunities** [critical hits](/Rules.aspx?ID=2190), [object immunities](/Rules.aspx?ID=2161), precision damage **Opening Shot** ([attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=540), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)) **Trigger** A creature enters area **A1**, and that creature isn’t wearing a _security badge_ and isn’t in the custody of a creature wearing a _security badge_; **Effect** All four prisms light up, shedding dim light in 15 feet. The trap makes one energy ray Strike against the triggering creature.
The trap then rolls initiative. --- **Routine** (4 actions) Each prism uses 1 action to make an energy ray Strike against the nearest creature who is not wearing a _security badge_ or in the custody of a creature wearing a _security badge_. This trap doesn’t take a multiple attack penalty. This trap loses 1 action for each prism disabled or destroyed. **Ranged** energy ray +15 ([range increment 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [versatile cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 1d6+5 fire --- **Reset** The trap deactivates and resets when there are no viable targets in area **A1**. Damaged prisms are fully repaired after 24 hours.
name: Prism Ray Trap

**Complexity** Simple

</column>

A rune hidden in the door’s decorative carvings delivers a telepathic jolt, draining will and muddling the mind.

---

**Stealth**
DC 28 (expert)

**Disable**
[Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) DC 26 (expert) to scratch out the rune without triggering a mental assault, or [_dispel magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1493) (3rd rank; [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) DC 24) to counteract the rune

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

**Telepathic Jolt** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)); **Trigger** A creature touches the door directly or with a tool; **Bypass** key; **Effect** The trap deals 6d10 mental damage to the triggering creature (DC 26 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Will save). On a failure, the target is additionally [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 1 for 24 hours (stupefied 3 on a critical failure).

---
**Reset**
The trap resets after 1 minute.
name: Meddler's Muddle

<title level="1" right="Hazard 7" pfs="">[Last Prayers of the Doomed](/Hazards.aspx?ID=539)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="Haunt" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=88" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #215: To Blot Out the Sun](/Sources.aspx?ID=259) pg. 15

**Complexity** Simple

</column>

Influenced by the last prayers of the dead, creatures experience extreme claustrophobia and batter at the walls and floor, desperate to escape, see the sun, and breathe fresh air—but their prayers go unanswered.

---

**Stealth**
DC 30 (expert) to hear desperate whispered prayers

**Disable**
[Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) DC 26 (expert) to disrupt the haunt through prayer, or [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) or [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) DC 27 (expert) to convince the spirits you’ll free them from this place

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

**Let Me Out!** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) **Trigger** A creature touches the altar, corpse, or statue; **Effect** The triggering creature batters their fists and body into the floor and walls, dealing 4d10+18 bludgeoning damage to themself (DC 25 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Will save). All creatures who witness this become [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 2 unless they succeed at a DC 25 Will save.

---
**Reset**
The haunt reforms after 1 day.
name: Last Prayers of the Doomed

**Source** [Pathfinder #215: To Blot Out the Sun](/Sources.aspx?ID=259) pg. 17 **Complexity** Complex Magical sensors trigger clockwork cryomisters to expel frigid air, freezing intruders and potentially encasing them in a block of ice. --- **Stealth** +26 (trained) **Disable** DC 22 [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) (expert) to disable the cryomisters, or DC 24 [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) (expert) to trick the magical sensors, or [_dispel magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1493) (3rd rank; [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) DC 24) to counteract the sensors **Cryo Mist** **Trigger** A creature enters the area, and that creature is not a clockwork soldier and is not openly wearing a [security badge](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3796); Effect The trap fills the area with frigid air, which deals 2d8+7 cold damage to creatures in the area (DC 22 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Fortitude save). Clockwork soldiers and any creatures openly wearing a _security badge_ are immune to this damage. The trap then rolls initiative. --- **Routine** (1 action) On its turn, the trap fills the area with frigid air, which deals 2d8+7 cold damage to creatures in the area. Creatures damaged must attempt a DC 22 Fortitude save. Clockwork soldiers and any creatures wearing a _security badge_ are immune to this damage. The accumulating ice causes the floor in the area to become [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2586) that lasts for 1 minute after the trap’s routine ends.
**Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target takes half damage and increases its [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) condition by 1.
**Failure** The target takes full damage and increases its slowed condition by 1.
**Critical Failure** The target takes double damage and increases its slowed condition by 2.
Once a creature’s actions are reduced to 0 by this slowed condition, the creature is completely encased in ice. The ice has [Hardness](/Rules.aspx?ID=2183) 5 and 20 Hit Points, and its DC to [Force Open](/Actions.aspx?ID=2375) is DC 22. Breaking the ice frees the creature and reduces their [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) condition by 1. Each round a creature is encased in ice, they take 1d8+7 cold damage (DC 22 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Fortitude save). --- **Reset** The trap resets after 10 minutes.
name: Ice Prison Trap

<title level="1" right="Hazard 5" pfs="">[Punish Defiance](/Hazards.aspx?ID=541)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Complex" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=30" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Trap" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=158" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #215: To Blot Out the Sun](/Sources.aspx?ID=259) pg. 19

**Complexity** Complex

</column>

A magical mural threatens uncooperative inmates, causing mental trauma.

---

**Stealth**
Stealth +23 (trained)

**Disable**
[Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) DC 26 (expert) to deactivate the mural, or [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) DC 26 (trained) to disrupt the mural’s magic, or [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) DC 24 (trained) to pose as a superior and order the mural to deactivate, or [_dispel magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1493) (3rd rank; [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) DC 24) to counteract the mural

<column gap="tiny">

**AC** 19, **Fort** +15, **Ref** +9

**Mural Hardness**
14

**Mural HP**
54 (BT 27)

**Immunities**
[critical hits](/Rules.aspx?ID=2190), [object immunities](/Rules.aspx?ID=2161), precision damage

</column>

**Final Warning** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) **Trigger** A creature defies the prison’s authority (such as by attacking a clockwork soldier or living graffiti); **Effect** The mural commands the inmate to cease their defiance and surrender to authority. All living creatures in the room must attempt a DC 24 Will save, becoming [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 1 and stupefied 1 on a failure, or frightened 2 and [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 2 on a critical failure. The trap then rolls initiative.

---
**Routine**
(1 action) On its turn, the trap threatens all living creatures in the room, dealing 3d6+5 mental damage (DC 22 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Will save).

---
**Reset**
1 hour.
name: Punish Defiance

<title level="1" right="Hazard 8" pfs="">[Stinkface Surprise](/Hazards.aspx?ID=542)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Mechanical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=105" />
<trait label="Trap" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=158" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #215: To Blot Out the Sun](/Sources.aspx?ID=259) pg. 25

**Complexity** Simple

</column>

A pile of excrement and rotten food is launched at whoever opens the trunk without first depressing a hidden button on the side of the trunk.

---

**Stealth**
DC 27 (expert; DC 31 to detect the hidden bypass button)

**Disable**
[Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) DC 26 (expert) to unhook the launch spring

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

**Stinkface Surprise** <actions string="Reaction" /> **Trigger** A creature opens the trunk or breaks open the trunk; **Bypass** hidden button; **Effect** The trap deals 4d10+22 poison damage to the triggering creature (DC 26 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Fortitude save). On a failure, the target is additionally [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=59) and [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) 4. A creature can spend one Interact action to scrub the filth from their eyes, removing the blinded condition. A creature can’t reduce the value of their sickened condition below 1 until they fully wash the filth off, a process which requires 5 minutes and suitable supplies.
name: Stinkface Surprise

<title level="1" right="Hazard 8" pfs="">[Furious Flame](/Hazards.aspx?ID=543)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Trap" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=158" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #215: To Blot Out the Sun](/Sources.aspx?ID=259) pg. 27

**Complexity** Simple

</column>

Religious sigils of Scal carefully carved into the doorframe capture Tokainen’s suppressed anger and frustration, unleashing it in a destructive explosion when a creature attempts to open the door.

---

**Stealth**
DC 28 (expert)

**Disable**
[Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) DC 26 (expert) to scratch out the religious iconography, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) DC 24 (trained) to pray for safe passage, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) DC 28 (expert) to soothe the emotions powering the magical ward, or [_dispel magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1493) (3rd rank; [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) DC 24) to counteract the magical energy

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

**Furious Explosion** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604)); **Trigger** A creature touches the door directly or with a tool; **Bypass** key; **Effect** The trap explodes in a 15-foot radius [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385), dealing 5d10 fire damage to all creatures in the area (DC 26 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). Creatures damaged by the trap must additionally succeed at a DC 26 Will save (against a [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) effect), becoming [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=63) for 1 minute on a failure.

---
**Reset**
The trap resets after 1 minute.
name: Furious Flame

<title level="1" right="Hazard 6" pfs="">[Acid Mist](/Hazards.aspx?ID=544)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Complex" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=30" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Trap" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=158" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #215: To Blot Out the Sun](/Sources.aspx?ID=259) pg. 34

**Complexity** Complex

</column>

A golden guardrail sprays magical, flesh-eating acid throughout the stairwell.

---

**Stealth**
+10 (trained)

**Disable**
[Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) DC 26 (expert) to deactivate the trap’s sensors, or [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) DC 24 (expert) to disrupt the guardrail’s magic, or [_dispel magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1493) (3rd rank; [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) DC 24) to counteract the guardrail

<column gap="tiny">

**AC** 21, **Fort** +17, **Ref** +11

**Guardrail Hardness**
13

**Guardrail HP**
56 (BT 28)

**Immunities**
[critical hits](/Rules.aspx?ID=2190), [object immunities](/Rules.aspx?ID=2161), precision damage

</column>

**Spray Acid** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=524), [arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534)); **Trigger** A creature steps on the south stairs; **Effect** The guardrail sprays flesh-eating acid, filling the entire stairwell. All living creatures on the stairs area take 2d8+9 acid damage (DC 27 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). The trap then rolls initiative.

---
**Routine**
(1 action) On its initiative, the trap continues to spray acid, dealing 2d8+9 acid damage to all living creatures on the stairs (DC 25 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save).

---
**Reset**
The trap automatically deactivates after 1 minute. It resets in 1 hour.
name: Acid Mist

<title level="1" right="Hazard 6" pfs="">[Blood Drain](/Hazards.aspx?ID=545)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Complex" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=30" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Trap" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=158" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #215: To Blot Out the Sun](/Sources.aspx?ID=259) pg. 37

**Complexity** Complex

</column>

A tiny spark of [Nin’s](/Deities.aspx?ID=353) will drains the blood from living intruders.

---

**Stealth**
+15 (trained)

**Disable**
[Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) DC 26 (expert) to vandalize the space, or [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) DC 24 (expert) to pray to a god for passage, or [_dispel magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1493) (3rd rank; [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) DC 25) to counteract the magical energy

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

**Drain Blood** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510)); **Trigger** A living creature enters the room of their own accord; **Effect** The trap drains the blood from all living creatures in the room, which manifests as a bloody mist in the air. All living creatures in the room are dealt 2d6+8 void damage (DC 26 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Fortitude save). On a failure, a creature increases the value of their [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) condition by 1. The trap then rolls initiative.

---
**Routine**
(1 action) On its initiative, the trap continues to drain blood, dealing 2d6+8 void damage to all living creatures in the room (DC 26 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Fortitude save). On a failure, a creature increases the value of their [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) condition by 1.

---
**Reset**
The trap automatically deactivates after 1 minute. It resets in 1 hour.
name: Blood Drain

<title level="1" right="Hazard 9" pfs="">[Conundrum Ward](/Hazards.aspx?ID=546)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Trap" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=158" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #215: To Blot Out the Sun](/Sources.aspx?ID=259) pg. 46

**Complexity** Simple

</column>

A complex geometrical ward with mathematical equations woven into its lines magically seals the door, harming anyone who touches it.

---

**Stealth**
DC 5

**Disable**
[Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) DC 30 (master) to sabotage the ward, [Geometry or Mathematics Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) DC 25 (trained) to calculate the solution to the complex equations, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) DC 28 (expert) to manipulate the ward’s precise underlying schematics, or [_dispel magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1493) (4th rank; [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) DC 26) to counteract the magical ward

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

**Mental Conundrum** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) **Trigger** A creature touches the door directly or with a tool; **Effect** The ward’s equations spiral and swirl, drawing the mind into an inescapable conundrum. The triggering creature is dealt 4d10+26 mental damage (DC 32 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Will save). On a failure, the target is additionally [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 2, or stupefied 4 on a critical failure.

---
**Reset**
The trap automatically resets after being triggered. If disabled, the ward does not reset.
name: Conundrum Ward

<title level="1" right="Hazard 9" pfs="">[Flensing Bone Pillars](/Hazards.aspx?ID=547)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Complex" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=30" />
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=48" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Trap" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=158" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #215: To Blot Out the Sun](/Sources.aspx?ID=259) pg. 49

**Complexity** Complex

</column>

Eight pillars composed of jagged bones spin, damaging anyone adjacent who doesn’t worship [Zon-Kuthon](/Deities.aspx?ID=296).

---

**Stealth**
+20 (master)

**Disable**
[Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) DC 22 to disassemble one pillar, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) DC 22 to ritually purify one pillar, or [_dispel magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1493) (4th rank; [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) DC 26) once to counteract the magic binding all the bone pillars together, deactivating the whole trap

<column gap="tiny">

**AC** 25, **Fort** +21, **Ref** +15

**Pillar Hardness**
6

**Pillar HP**
24 (BT 12)

**Immunities**
[critical hits](/Rules.aspx?ID=2190), [object immunities](/Rules.aspx?ID=2161), precision damage

**Weaknesses**
[holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517) 5

</column>

**Spin** <actions string="Reaction" /> **Trigger** A creature who doesn’t worship Zon-Kuthon and who isn’t granted immunity by a velstrac enters the area; **Effect** The bones composing the pillars begin to spin, then makes a bone spur attack against one creature adjacent to any pillar. The trap then rolls initiative.

---
**Routine**
(8 actions) Each of the functioning bone pillars makes a bone spur Strike against an adjacent creature. This trap doesn’t take a multiple attack penalty. This trap loses one action for each disabled or destroyed pillar.

**Melee** <actions string="Single Action" /> bone spur +21, **Damage** 2d8+13 slashing plus 1d4 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86)

---
**Reset**
The trap deactivates and resets after 1 minute. The trap is automatically destroyed if all the velstracs on this floor are killed.
name: Flensing Bone Pillars
search_markdown:
<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Complex" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=30" />
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=48" />

<title level="1" right="Hazard 9" pfs="">[Silvered Retribution](/Hazards.aspx?ID=548)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Complex" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=30" />
<trait label="Mechanical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=105" />
<trait label="Trap" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=158" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #215: To Blot Out the Sun](/Sources.aspx?ID=259) pg. 51

**Complexity** Complex

</column>

Pressure plates in the floor, wall, and ceiling of this hall trigger silver blades to protrude from the ceiling, slashing anything passing through the hall.

---

**Stealth**
+23 (master)

**Disable**
[Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) DC 28 (master) to disable the pressure plates; two successes are required to disable the trap

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

**Sudden Strikes** <actions string="Reaction" /> **Trigger** A creature steps on the floor or places pressure on the walls or ceiling of these two adjoining halls; **Effect** Silver blades erupt from the walls and wildly slash everything in the area, dealing 2d10+13 slashing damage ([magic](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2920)) to all creatures in the area (DC 30 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). The trap then rolls initiative.

---
**Routine**
(2 actions) The maze of blades continues to spin, jab, and slash, dealing 2d10+13 slashing damage ([magic](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2920)) to all creatures in the area (DC 28 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save).

---
**Reset**
The trap deactivates after 1 minute and can be manually reset.
name: Silvered Retribution

<title level="1" right="Hazard 8" pfs="">[Electric Fence](/Hazards.aspx?ID=549)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Electricity" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=56" />
<trait label="Mechanical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=105" />
<trait label="Trap" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=158" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #215: To Blot Out the Sun](/Sources.aspx?ID=259) pg. 54

**Complexity** Simple

</column>

A locked, wrought iron fence with bars spaced inches apart is magically electrified.

---

**Stealth**
DC 30 (expert)

**Disable**
[Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) or [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) DC 28 (expert) to disconnect the fence from its magitech power source

<column gap="tiny">

**AC** 25, **Fort** +20, **Ref** +14

**Hardness**
15

**HP**
60 (BT 30)

**Immunities**
[object immunities](/Rules.aspx?ID=2161)

</column>

**Shock** <actions string="Reaction" /> **Trigger** A creature touches the fence directly or with a tool or weapon; **Effect** The fence deals 6d12 electricity damage to the triggering creature (DC 26 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex).

---
**Routine**

---
**Reset**
The trap automatically resets. If deactivated or disabled, the trap doesn’t reset.

Then, the trap summons 2 [bulbous blood oozes](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3887), which attack living creatures.

<title level="1" right="Hazard 8" pfs="">[Paralysis Trap](/Hazards.aspx?ID=551)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #215: To Blot Out the Sun](/Sources.aspx?ID=259) pg. 61

**Complexity** Simple

</column>

Magical symbols carved in the doorframe magically ward this door; anyone attempting to pick the lock is paralyzed and wracked with pain. Disabling or breaking the trap does not disable or break the lock.

---

**Stealth**
DC 22 (trained)

**Disable**
**Thievery** DC 28 (expert) to scratch out the magical symbols, **Arcana** DC 28 (expert) to safely siphon the magical energy, or [_dispel magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1493) (4th rank; [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) DC 26); **Bypass** Anyone other than Mirida turns the door handle three times to the right and once to the left in quick succession.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

**Debilitating Pain** <actions string="Reaction" /> **Trigger** A creature tries to unlock or [Pick the Lock](/Actions.aspx?ID=2412); **Effect** The trap deals the triggering creature 2d10+11 nonlethal mental damage (DC 30 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Will save) and 1d6 [persistent nonlethal mental damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86). A creature that fails their save is additionally [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85) for as long as they suffer the persistent mental damage.

---
**Reset**
The trap automatically resets. It doesn’t reset if the trap is disabled.
name: Paralysis Trap

<title level="1" right="Hazard 9" pfs="">[Mournful Petals Haunt](/Hazards.aspx?ID=552)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Complex" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=30" />
<trait label="Haunt" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=88" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #215: To Blot Out the Sun](/Sources.aspx?ID=259) pg. 62

**Complexity** Complex

</column>

A flurry of scattered flower petals flies through the area, as psychic impressions of pain, sorrow, and being trapped assail the living—Mirida’s scattered memories.

---

**Stealth**
+26 (expert)

**Disable**
DC 31 [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) or [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) (expert) to weaken the haunt, DC 29 [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) or [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) (expert) to soothe the haunt’s sorrow; three total successes in any combination are required to disable the haunt.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

**Scattered Memories** <actions string="Reaction" /> **Trigger** Two or more creatures enter the area; **Effect** Fragments of Mirida’s scattered thoughts and memories manifest in the room as flower petals, the sounds of weeping, whimpers of pain, and shrieks echo through the area, dealing 2d10+13 mental damage to all intelligent creatures in the area (DC 32 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Will save). The haunt then rolls initiative.

---
**Routine**
(1 action) The flower petals surge through the room, dealing 2d10+13 slashing damage to all creatures in the area (DC 32 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). A creature that fails is additionally [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 2 (frightened 4 on a critical failure).

---
**Reset**
The petals disperse after 1 minute and reset after 1 day.
name: Mournful Petals Haunt

**Source** [Pathfinder #215: To Blot Out the Sun](/Sources.aspx?ID=259) pg. 65 **Complexity** Complex The _shadow beacon’s_ operation causes harmful energy to blast out of the base of the device. --- **Stealth** + 18 **Disable** DC 27 [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) or [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) (master) to rig a dispersal system, or DC 27 [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43), [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44), or [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) (master) to safely vent the magical energy; four total successes in any combination to disable **Priming** ([cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555), [shadow](/Traits.aspx?ID=693)) **Trigger** Nizca and Deg activate the shadow beacon; **Effect** The shadow beacon sparks to life. The trap then rolls initiative. --- **Routine** (1 action) The shadow beacon fires shadows into the sky, discharging excess energy out of the device’s lower half. The trap deals 4d6 cold and 4d6 void damage to all non-shadow creatures in a 60-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) from the base of the device (DC 26 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Fortitude save). For each successful check to disable the trap, the cold and void damage dealt by the trap on subsequent rounds are both reduced by 1d6 damage. A creature that fails their save is pushed back 10 feet (15 feet on a critical failure).

**Special** Disabling the shadow discharge does not disable the shadow beacon.
name: Shadow Discharge

**Source** [Pathfinder #215: To Blot Out the Sun](/Sources.aspx?ID=259) pg. 66 **Complexity** Complex The _shadow beacon_ malfunctions, firing bolts of electricity across the battlefield. --- **Stealth** + 17 **Disable** DC 25 [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) or [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) (master) to ground the device, or DC 25 [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43), [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44), or [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) (master) to safely vent the magical energy; three total successes in any combination to disable **Shockwave** ([sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=697)) **Trigger** The fourth round of combat begins; **Effect** The _shadow beacon_ malfunctions, releasing a shock wave of energy. All creatures within 100 feet of the _shadow beacon_ must succeed at a DC 29 Reflex save or be knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88). Creatures who are flying are instead knocked down 15 feet on a failure, taking 1d10+5 bludgeoning damage and landing prone if they collide with a solid object during this movement. The trap then rolls initiative. --- **Routine** (3 actions) The shadow beacon makes three electric bolt Strikes at random creatures within 100 feet. One of these Strikes targets either Deg or Nizca, and the other two target PCs. The trap loses 1 action for each successful disable.
Melee electric bolt +22, Damage 2d10+9 electricity; no multiple attack penalty
name: Shocking Malfunction

<title level="1" right="Hazard 5" pfs="">[Precarious Footing](/Hazards.aspx?ID=555)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Environmental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=591" />
<trait label="Fire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=604" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #217: Death Sails a Wine-Dark Sea
](/Sources.aspx?ID=273) pg. 20

**Complexity** Simple

</column>

The ground around the lava pit is crumbling and unstable.

---

**Stealth**
DC 24 (trained)

**Disable**
DC 18 [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) (trained) to weather the collapse safely; DC 20 [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) (trained) to stabilize the ground

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

**Lava Bath** <actions string="Reaction" /> **Trigger** A creature steps into the pit or within 5 feet of its edge; **Effect** The ground crumbles apart and the creature slides into the lava. That creature can use the [Grab an Edge](/Actions.aspx?ID=2313) reaction to avoid falling. Otherwise it falls 20 feet (taking 10 fall damage) and is exposed to lava, taking 6d6 fire damage per round of contact. Climbing the crumbling rock face requires a successful DC 20 [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) check.
name: Precarious Footing

**Cruel Temptation** claw +20, **Damage** 2d10+10 mental --- **Special** The hazard continues each round until defeated, and the hallway’s end cannot be reached until the hazard is stopped.

<title level="1" right="Hazard 8" pfs="">[Rage of the Sky Father](/Hazards.aspx?ID=557)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="Complex" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=30" />
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=48" />
<trait label="Trap" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=158" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #218: Titanbane
](/Sources.aspx?ID=274) pg. 7

**Complexity** Complex

</column>

Ominous storm clouds gather overhead, pulsating with flashes of blue-white lightning.

---

**Stealth**
+18 (expert)

**Disable**
DC 23 [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) (trained) to petition [Gozreh](/Deities.aspx?ID=284) for safe passage or 28 [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43), or [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) (expert) to disperse the storm’s magical energies. A PC whose voice is exceptionally loud (such as using the [_bullhorn_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1971) cantrip) can also attempt to [Disable](/Actions.aspx?ID=2411) this hazard with a DC 25 [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) check to convince the Gozren priests that they mean no harm; the priests make a second prayer to dissipate the hazard on the following round, though they’re unable to stop the sargassum heaps.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

**Thunderclap** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=697)) **Trigger** A creature not designated by Katlanthe approaches within 10 feet of the circle of stone columns; **Effect** The storm clouds release a boom of thunder. Creatures atop the plateau take 2d10+9 sonic damage (DC 26 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Fortitude save). On a critical failure, the creature is also [deafened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=66) for 1d4 rounds. The hazard rolls initiative.

---
**Routine**
(1 action) ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586)) A bolt of lightning strikes a 10-foot [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385) atop the plateau of the GM’s choosing. Creatures in the area take 2d10+9 electricity damage (DC 28 Reflex save). On a critical failure, the creature is also [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=93) 1. Creatures designated by Katlanthe within the area are infused with divine energy for 1 round. They gain the electricity trait and [immunity](/Rules.aspx?ID=2312) to electricity damage, and all their attacks deal an additional 1d6 electricity damage.

---
**Reset**
The storm clouds disperse once no creature other than those designated by Katlanthe are atop the plateau.
name: Rage of the Sky Father

**Source** [Pathfinder #218: Titanbane ](/Sources.aspx?ID=274) pg. 10 **Complexity** Complex A shadow the size of an island appears in the water beneath the ship. --- **Stealth** +19 (expert) **Disable** DC 27 [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34), [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36), or [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) or DC 23 [Sailing Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) to help the crew of the _Tide Hunter_ escape the Ketos; three total successes are required to disable the hazard **AC** 25, **Fort** +20, **Ref** +12 **Tentacle HP** 60 **Grasp of the Depths** **Trigger** The Tide Hunter enters the Ketos’s hunting grounds; **Effect** The Ketos uses its eight tentacles to seize the ship, holding it in place. The hazard then rolls initiative. --- **Routine** (3 actions) The Ketos uses its actions to make tentacle Strikes against creatures aboard the _Tide Hunter_ or within 30 feet of the ship.
**Melee** tentacle +18, **Damage** 2d10+9 slashing; no multiple attack penalty --- **Reset** If three or more of its tentacles are destroyed, the Ketos releases the _Tide Hunter_ and disappears into the ocean’s depths to nurse its wounds. The monster does not return to this area for 24 hours.
name: The Ketos

**Source** [Pathfinder #218: Titanbane ](/Sources.aspx?ID=274) pg. 15 **Complexity** Complex The spirits of the island’s citizens manifest and panic as the vault fills with seawater. --- **Stealth** +22 (expert) to sense the terror of the spirits inside the vault **Disable** DC 33 [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) (trained) twice to [Force Open](/Actions.aspx?ID=2375) the vault’s door or DC 30 [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) (trained) three times to exorcise the spirits **Flooding Vault** **Trigger** A living creature spends more than 1 minute inside the vault; **Effect** The vault’s door slams shut, trapping any creatures inside. Meanwhile, the souls of the dozens of citizens who died when Pakilykos sank manifest throughout the vault. The haunt then rolls initiative. --- **Routine** (1 action) Water begins to fill the vault from a crack in the floor hidden beneath the bones. The spirits of the citizens panic and grasp at the living, begging them to act. Each creature in the vault takes 2d10+13 void damage with a basic DC 28 Fortitude saving throw. On a failure, the creature is also [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) 1 (or enfeebled 2 on a critical failure).
The first time the hazard takes this action, water fills the vault to knee-height, making the area difficult terrain. The second time the hazard takes this action, the vault fills halfway with water, and creatures inside must succeed at a DC 15 [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) check each round to Swim. The third time the hazard takes this action, the vault fills entirely with water, and creatures inside must hold their breath. --- **Reset** The waters recede and the spirits disappear once no living creatures remain inside the vault, but the haunt resets immediately, triggering again if a living creature steps inside.
name: Vault-Drowned Souls

**Source** [Pathfinder #218: Titanbane ](/Sources.aspx?ID=274) pg. 30 **Complexity** Complex Ekriathe’s talon releases a pulse of psychic rage throughout the area, transforming the lighthouse’s lantern room into a pitched battlefield. --- **Stealth** +22 (expert) **Disable** DC 27 [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) (expert) twice to disrupt the divine mythic energies, or DC 32 [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) (expert) twice to cut down the talon and remove it from the room without triggering the haunt **AC** 30, **Fort** +22, **Ref** +16 **Hardness** 10 **HP** 70 (BT 35) **Immunities** [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) **Weaknesses** [area](/Rules.aspx?ID=2384) damage 10 **The Battle for Dhuraxilis** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=629)) **Trigger** A creature other than Dorlina touches Ekriathe’s talon; **Effect** Each creature in the area sees the lantern room transform into a battlefield, where Ekriathe and her allies battle a force of human soldiers. Each creature takes 4d12+26 mental damage (DC 33 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Will save). A creature that fails or critically fails the save believes it has been transported backward in time and can’t willingly leave the lantern room until the haunt is disabled. An affected creature can attempt a new save at the end of each of its turns to end this effect. The haunt then rolls initiative. --- **Routine** (3 actions) The haunt attacks creatures in the area, making spear and talon Strikes against targets determined at random. The haunt takes the multiple attack penalty only if it targets the same creature more than once on a turn. The haunt loses one action if its Hit Points are reduced below its Break Threshold.
**Melee** spear +23, **Damage** 2d12+13 mental
**Melee** talon +23 ([agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526)), **Damage** 2d10+13 mental --- **Reset** The haunt deactivates once no creatures remain in the lantern room, but it resets immediately.
name: Ekriathe's Rage

**Source** [Pathfinder #218: Titanbane ](/Sources.aspx?ID=274) pg. 54 **Complexity** Simple The Granite Ox shakes off centuries of accumulated earth, flinging the PCs toward its hindquarters. --- **Stealth** DC 10 **Shake** **Trigger** The Granite Ox awakens from slumber; **Effect** Creatures in area **F2** are thrown hundreds of feet from the peak of the Ox's horn onto its back. Each creature takes falling damage as if they fell 60 feet (30 bludgeoning damage), with a 31 Reflex save. Creatures can arrest their fall with spells like gentle landing, as normal. In addition, the result determines how far the PC must travel in order to escape the Ox's tail in the Swatted Like Flies hazard.
**Critical Success** The PC must travel 20 feet.
**Success** The PC must travel 40 feet.
**Failure** The PC must travel 60 feet.
**Critical Failure** As failure, and the PC is [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=93) 1.
name: Shaken Loose

summary_markdown: The Granite Ox shakes off centuries of accumulated earth, flinging the PCs toward its hindquarters.

markdown:
**Source** [Pathfinder #218: Titanbane ](/Sources.aspx?ID=274) pg. 54 **Complexity** Complex Feeling the presence of creatures on its backside, the Granite Ox swishes its stone tail to swat them away.
**Escaping** The PCs overcome this hazard by moving out of the area that the Ox’s tail can reach. A PC escapes (and is not subject to tail Strikes) by ascending the Ox’s sloped back, traveling a distance based on the result of the PC’s Reflex save from the Shaken Loose hazard. PCs can [Climb](/Actions.aspx?ID=2374) up the Ox’s back with a successful DC 24 [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) check or—by finding and scrambling up improvised paths—a DC 26 [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34), [Perception](/Rules.aspx?ID=2298), or [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) check. With a successful DC 20 Acrobatics check, a flying PC can ascend half their fly Speed (or their full Speed on a critical success). PCs can attempt to Disable the hazard whether or not they’re in range of the tail. --- **Stealth** +18 (expert) **Disable** DC 26 [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) to scramble out of the way, DC 24 [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) or [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) (trained) to draw the Ox’s attention. The DC of the [flat check](/Rules.aspx?ID=333) for this round (see Routine) is increased by 4 on a success, or by 8 on a critical success. Increasing the flat check DC to 21 or higher buys the party enough time to move up the Ox’s back, disabling the hazard. **Tail Swat** **Trigger** A creature lands near the base of the Granite Ox’s tail; **Effect** The Granite Ox makes a tail Strike against the triggering creature. The hazard then rolls initiative. --- **Routine** (1 action) The Granite Ox makes a tail Strike against one creature in the area. If the attack succeeds, the GM rolls a DC 9 [flat check](/Rules.aspx?ID=333). On a success, the hazard makes another Strike. The hazard continues making Strikes until the GM fails the flat check.
**Melee** tail +20, Damage 2d10+11 bludgeoning plus [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) 1 for 1 round; no multiple attack penalty --- **Reset** Once all of the PCs moved outside the tail’s striking range, the hazard is effectively disabled. The hazard reactivates if any PC returns to that area.
name: Swatted Like Flies

**Source** [Pathfinder #218: Titanbane ](/Sources.aspx?ID=274) pg. 55 **Complexity** Complex The head of the Granite Ox tosses back and forth as the creature charges toward Dhuraxilis. --- **Stealth** +22 (master) **Disable** DC 27 [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) (master) to tug one of the ox’s ears, DC 32 [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43), [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44), or [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) (expert) to distract the Ox with a display of magic, or DC 35 [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) (expert) to dangle in front of the Ox’s eyes and obscure its vision; three successes are required to disable the hazard **Toss** **Trigger** A creature reaches the Granite Ox’s head or attempts a skill check to disable the hazard; **Effect** The Granite Ox tosses its head. Creatures in the area 2d12+13 bludgeoning damage must attempt a DC 29 Fortitude saving throw. The hazard rolls initiative.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature takes half damage.
**Failure** The creature takes full damage and is knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88).
**Critical Failure** The creature takes double damage, is knocked prone, and takes 1d12 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) as it scrapes across the Ox’s scales. --- **Routine** (1 action) The Granite Ox bucks. Each creature on the Ox’s head must attempt a DC 33 Reflex save. [Prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88) creatures gain a +2 circumstance bonus to the save. On a failure, the creature falls prone. On a critical failure, the creature flies from the Ox’s back, falling 120 feet to the ground or into Kardaji Bay. --- **Reset** The hazard ends automatically after 5 rounds or once all PCs are thrown from the Ox’s back, whichever comes first.
name: By the Horns

**Source** [Pathfinder #218: Titanbane ](/Sources.aspx?ID=274) pg. 61 **Complexity** Complex The pooling seawater burbles and hisses. --- **Stealth** +17 (expert) **Disable** DC 32 [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) or [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) (master) to dissipate the fire-aligned energies in the water, or DC 28 [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) or [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) to beseech Sangeh and convince her to momentarily tamp down her mythic power; two successes are required to disable the hazard **Scalding Gout** ([fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=732)) **Trigger** A creature within the hazard’s area rolls initiative; **Effect** An enormous column of superheated steam rises from the water, dealing 4d12+26 fire damage to all creatures in the area (DC 28 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). On a critical failure, the creature also takes 1d12 [persistent fire damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) as the water continues to burn their skin. The hazard then rolls initiative. --- **Routine** (2 actions) The hazard uses each of its actions to create a Blistering Plume centered on a space chosen by the GM. Each successful check to disable the hazard removes one action from this routine.
**Blistering Plume** A 30-foot-tall geyser erupts from the boiling water. Creatures in a 5-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) of the geyser take 2d12+13 fire damage (DC 30 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save).
name: Sudden Geysers

**Source** [Pathfinder #163: Ruins of Gauntlight](/Sources.aspx?ID=71) pg. 9 **Complexity** Complex A half-dozen ghostly [kobolds](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=435) rise from the rubble in a howling vortex. --- **Stealth** +11 (trained) **Disable** DC 18 [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) (trained) to frighten the spirits with a threatening display, or DC 21 [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) (trained) to exorcise the spirits **Confusing Confrontation** ([emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) **Trigger** A creature enters a square either fully or partially filled with rubble in the middle of the room; Effect Six ghostly kobolds surge out of the rubble with eerie yowls. Each creature in area A7 must attempt a DC 18 Will save with the following results. The haunt then rolls initiative. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected, is temporarily immune to Confusing Confrontation for 24 hours, and realizes that a display of intimidating dominance might quell the ghostly kobolds’ assault.
**Success** The creature is [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 1.
**Failure** The creature is [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=63) for 1 round and is frightened 2.
**Critical Failure** The creature is confused for 2 rounds and is frightened 3. --- **Routine** (1 action) The spirits swoop together toward one creature in area **A7** who’s [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76), instilling feelings of betrayal and confusion. The target takes 1d10+4 [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) damage (DC 18 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Will save).
name: Stonescale Spirits

**Source** [Pathfinder #163: Ruins of Gauntlight](/Sources.aspx?ID=71) pg. 13 **Complexity** Complex A bloody image of Belcorra arises, emits a soul‑draining light, then inhales blood from living creatures in the room. --- **Stealth** +13 (expert) **Disable** DC 22 [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) (trained) to wipe away enough of the bloodstain to disrupt the haunt’s necromantic energies, or DC 20 [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) (trained) to exorcise the haunt **AC** 19, **Fort** +6, **Ref** +9, **Will** +12 **Immunities** [critical hits](/Rules.aspx?ID=2314), [object immunities](/Rules.aspx?ID=2161), precision damage **Weaknesses** positive 5 **Resistances** physical 5 (except [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2840)) **Gauntlight Beam** ([light](/Traits.aspx?ID=640), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) **Trigger** A living creature ends its turn in area A11 at night; **Effect** A ghastly image of Belcorra, seemingly composed of fresh blood, rises from the bloodstain on the floor and rotates in place like a lighthouse. Beams of cold blue light shine from Belcorra’s eyes and open mouth, causing blisters and decay to spread across flesh. Living creatures within a 30-foot [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385) from the center of area A11 must attempt a DC 20 Fortitude save. The haunt then rolls initiative.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature takes 1d8 negative damage.
**Failure** The creature takes 1d8+6 negative damage and is [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65) for 1 round.
**Critical Failure** The creature takes 2d8+6 negative damage and is [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=59) for 1 round then dazzled for 1 round. --- **Routine** (1 action) The bloody image opens its mouth and appears to inhale, drawing blood from the body of one creature in its line of sight within 90 feet from the center of area A11. The creature targeted must attempt a DC 20 Fortitude save.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature takes 1d10 [bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) damage.
**Failure** The creature takes 1d10+6 bleed damage.
**Critical Failure** The creature takes 2d10+6 bleed damage and is [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) 2 as long as it’s bleeding. --- **Reset** If the haunt is disabled or destroyed through damage, the bloody phantom of Belcorra sprays upward in a spiral, dousing the trap door leading to area **A25** before draining upward through the keyhole, which reduces the Thievery DC to [Pick the Lock](/Actions.aspx?ID=2412) to 20. At the next sunrise, the blood drains back down to this room and the haunt resets. The haunt is destroyed permanently once Lasda Venkervale is rescued from area **D9**.
name: Blood of Belcorra

**Ranged** apocalypse beam +16; **Damage** 2d10+13 damage (fire damage from the burning city **[a]**, bludgeoning damage from the tsunami **[b]**, sonic damage from the earthquake **[c]**, mental damage from the monster **[d]**, and negative damage from the undead uprising **[e]**) --- **Reset** Each trap resets automatically after 1 hour.

**Source** [Pathfinder #163: Ruins of Gauntlight](/Sources.aspx?ID=71) pg. 37 **Complexity** Complex An overwhelming feeling of being watched wells up in the minds of those in the room, an instant before an eerie red eye opens in the western wall. --- **Stealth** +12 (expert) **Disable** DC 22 [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) (trained) to appear uninteresting to the watching eye (and thus be ignored by it) or DC 22 [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) (trained) to ward against being seen by or affected by the eye **AC** 21, **Fort** +14, **Ref** +8, **Will** +8 **Hardness** 12 **HP** 50 (BT 25) **Immunities** [critical hits](/Rules.aspx?ID=2314), [object immunities](/Rules.aspx?ID=2161), precision damage **Weaknesses** positive 5 **Someone is Watching** ([emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) **Trigger** A living creature remains in area **C4** for at least 1 round; **Effect** An overwhelming wave of paranoia fills area **C4**. Each creature in the room must attempt a DC 25 Will save, with the following results. An eerie red eye then opens in the center of the western wall, looking about the room, and the haunt rolls initiative.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature becomes [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 1 and feels like someone or something is watching them for as long as they remain frightened.
**Failure** The creature becomes frightened 2 and treats no one as an ally as long as they remain frightened.
**Critical Failure** The creature becomes frightened 3 and is [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=63) as long as they remain frightened. --- **Routine** (1 action) The eye glances about, and those it can see (whether in area **C4** or outside of it) take 4d6 mental damage (DC 21 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Will save) as fears of being watched impart ripples of pain. A creature that takes mental damage from this effect doesn’t reduce their frightened value at the end of their next turn. --- **Reset** The haunt becomes inert at the end of any round in which there are no frightened creatures it can see. It stays dormant for 1 hour, after which point it resets.
name: Watching wall

**Source** [Pathfinder #163: Ruins of Gauntlight](/Sources.aspx?ID=71) pg. 46 **Complexity** Complex The hatch atop the haunted furnace flips open and disgorges a pair of shrieking, burning ghosts. --- **Stealth** +15 (expert) **Disable** DC 22 [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) (trained) to cow one of the vengeful spirits or DC 25 [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) (trained) to exorcise the spirit. The haunt remains active until both spirits are cowed or exorcised, or until the furnace is destroyed. **AC** 21, **Fort** +15, **Ref** +8 **Hardness** 13 **HP** 60 (BT 30) **Immunities** [critical hits](/Rules.aspx?ID=2314), [object immunities](/Rules.aspx?ID=2161), precision damage **Weaknesses** [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555) 8, positive 5 **Ghostly Assault** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647))**Trigger** A living creature with an Intelligence score of 15 or higher enters the room, or any creature touches the furnace; **Effect** Burning ghosts burst from the furnace, exposing the triggering creature to the haunt’s burn knowledge effect. The haunt rolls initiative.
Burn **Knowledge** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) The target of the haunt’s initial Ghostly Assault, as well as any creature later hit by a burning lash Strike, loses random memories, as if these thoughts were incinerated like pages in a burning book. The creature must attempt a DC 23 Will save.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature becomes [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 1 for 1 minute while they forget random memories, as if these thoughts were incinerated like pages in a burning book.
**Failure** As success, but the stupefied 1 condition persists for 24 hours.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but stupefied 2. --- **Routine** (3 actions) The burning ghosts lash at a random [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) creature in the room (or any random creature, if no creatures in the room are stupefied).
**Ranged** burning lash +14 ([fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [range 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 2d6 fire plus 2d6 mental and burn knowledge --- **Reset** The haunt resets 1 hour after there are no creatures in the room.
name: Vengeful Furnace

<title level="1" right="Hazard 6" pfs="">[Rusty Grate Pit](/Hazards.aspx?ID=574)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Mechanical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=105" />
<trait label="Trap" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=158" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #164: Hands of the Devil](/Sources.aspx?ID=75) pg. 12

**Complexity** Simple

</column>

The rusty grate covers a pit that’s 10 feet in diameter and 40 feet deep.

---

**Stealth**
DC 0

**Disable**
DC 18 [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) to harmlessly trigger the trap by nudging the gate or the dangling winch, or DC 24 Thievery to stabilize the grate so it doesn’t collapse.

<column gap="tiny">

**AC** 21, **Fort** +17, **Ref** +11

**Hardness**
14

**HP**
56 (BT 28)

**Immunities**
[critical hits](/Rules.aspx?ID=2314), [object immunities](/Rules.aspx?ID=2161), precision damage

</column>

**Pitfall** <actions string="Reaction" /> **Trigger** A creature walks onto the grate; **Effect** The triggering creature falls in and takes falling damage (20 bludgeoning damage). That creature can use the [Grab an Edge](/Actions.aspx?ID=2313) reaction to avoid falling. Whether or not the creature Grabs an Edge, the rusty winch above crashes down atop the triggering creature, dealing 3d10 bludgeoning damage and dislodging its grip (DC 24 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). The creature can still try to Grab an Edge again.
name: Rusty Grate Pit

**Source** [Pathfinder #164: Hands of the Devil](/Sources.aspx?ID=75) pg. 21 **Complexity** Complex Six long blades, hidden in grooves in the walls and floor, zigzag through different parts of this hallway when any pressure plate in the hallway intersection is depressed; there are so many plates it’s impossible to avoid them when moving through the room. The blades retreat into the floor and move through the hidden grooves before swinging out from the wall again in a different location. --- **Stealth** DC +18 (expert) **Disable** DC 26 [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) (expert) to disable each blade, or utter the magical passphrase (which only Chafkhem knows) to deactivate the trap for 10 minutes **AC** 27, **Fort** +19, **Ref** +13 **Scythe Blade Hardness** 16 **Scythe Blade HP** 30 (BT 15) **Immunities** [critical hits](/Rules.aspx?ID=2314), [object immunities](/Rules.aspx?ID=2161), precision damage **Dicing Scythes** **Trigger** A creature steps in the 15-footby- 25-foot area where the branching hallways connect; Effect The trap uses Scythe Shuffle; each blade makes a scythe Strike against each creature in its region, then it uses Scythe Shuffle again. The trap then rolls for initiative.
Scythe Shuffle The blades travel erratically throughout the hallway’s branches, out of sight under the floors or behind the walls. For each blade, roll 1d4 to determine the region in which it next makes scythe Strikes. A creature can [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=2301) (DC 22) to learn clues about blades in the region they’re currently occupying. On a success, the creature knows how many blades are currently in its region.
1. Main intersection (the 15-foot-by-25-foot area where the hallways connect, as marked on area **B20**)
2. North branch (from the main intersection to the secret door to area **B14**)
3. Central hall (from the main intersection to the secret door to area **B24**)
4. South branch (from the main intersection to the wall shared with area **B25**) --- **Routine** (7 actions) The trap spends 1 action for each of its blades; a blade makes a scythe Strike against each creature in its region. With its final action, the trap uses Scythe Shuffle. Reduce the number of actions the trap can take by 1 for each destroyed blade.
**Melee** scythe +20 ([deadly 1d12](/Traits.aspx?ID=570)), **Damage** 1d12+8 slashing; no multiple attack penalty --- **Reset** The trap resets when no creatures remain in area **B20**. Damaged or destroyed blades aren’t repaired when the trap resets.
name: Shuffling Scythe Blades
