**Source** [Pathfinder #164: Hands of the Devil](/Sources.aspx?ID=75) pg. 52 **Complexity** Complex When any creature other than a devil enters the light, the magic interwoven in the light holds the creature in place and rings an alarm. --- **Stealth** DC +18 (expert) **Disable** **Disable** DC 26 [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) (master) to distort or diffuse the light, keeping the light cone intact but preventing the trap from triggering, or [_dispel magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1493) (4th level; counteract DC 26) to dispel the light, leaving the room in darkness **Stasis Field** ([enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)); **Trigger** A non-[devil](/Traits.aspx?ID=575) creature moves into the light; **Effect** The light expands to fill the room, and each nondevil creature within the room must attempt a DC 26 Will save. A high-pitched chime sounds in the barracks (area **D15**), audible in this room as well. The trap then rolls initiative.
**Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target is [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=93) 2.
**Failure** The target is [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85) for 1 round.
**Critical Failure** The target is [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 2 for 1d4 rounds and paralyzed for 1 round. --- **Routine** (1 action) All [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=93) and [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85) creatures in the room take 1d10 mental damage (DC 26 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Will save). Creatures who fail also become paralyzed for 1 round but can use a reaction to mentally fight off this stasis; creatures who do so take 5d10 mental damage but are no longer paralyzed. --- **Reset** The stasis magic in the light builds up over the course of an hour, after which the trap can trigger again
name: Paralyzing Light Trap

<title level="1" right="Hazard 8" pfs="">[Painful Suggestion Trap](/Hazards.aspx?ID=577)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Trap" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=158" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #164: Hands of the Devil](/Sources.aspx?ID=75) pg. 54

**Complexity** Simple

</column>

A hidden rune on the floor under the filth, just past the threshold, triggers an ersatz ghost.

---

**Stealth**
DC 26 (expert)

**Disable**
DC 28 [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) (expert) to remove the rune without triggering it, or [_dispel magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1493) (4th level; [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) DC 26) to dispel the rune

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

**Counterfeit Haunting** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631), [linguistic](/Traits.aspx?ID=642), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) **Trigger** A living creature moves over the rune; **Effect** A frightful moan echoes through the room, and a hoarse telepathic voice shouts, “Get out!” This shout deals 6d12 mental damage to creatures in the room (DC 26 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Will save). Creatures who fail this saving throw must immediately leave the room and can’t willingly reenter it for 1 minute (1 hour on a critical failure).

---
**Reset**
The trap automatically resets after 1 hour.
name: Painful Suggestion Trap

<title level="1" right="Hazard 10" pfs="">[Daemonic Fog](/Hazards.aspx?ID=578)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Environmental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=62" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #165: Eyes of Empty Death](/Sources.aspx?ID=81) pg. 38

**Complexity** Simple

</column>

The fog solidifies into tiny, gnawing creatures that devour everyone within before fading back into mist.

---

**Stealth**
DC 30 (expert)

**Disable**
DC 28 [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) (expert) to disrupt the fog before the hazard triggers or [_dispel magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1493) (5th level; [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) DC 26) to counteract the magic.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

**Gnawing Fog** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65)) **Trigger** A creature crosses the cavern’s midpoint (the dotted line on the map); **Effect** The hazard deals 9d6 piercing damage (DC 32 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save) to the triggering creature and all other non‑fiend creatures within 20 feet. A creature that critically fails the saving throw is [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=63) for 1d4 rounds.

---
**Reset**
The hazard resets over the course of an hour as the daemonic stain reenergizes the fog.
name: Daemonic Fog

<title level="1" right="Hazard 10" pfs="">[Blast Tumbler](/Hazards.aspx?ID=580)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Mechanical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=105" />
<trait label="Trap" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=158" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #165: Eyes of Empty Death](/Sources.aspx?ID=81) pg. 45

**Complexity** Simple

</column>

A hard-to-reach rune is placed on the lock’s tumbler. It emits a blast of force when the door is jostled.

---

**Stealth**
DC 32 (master)

**Disable**
DC 32 [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) (master) to disarm the rune, or [_dispel magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1493) (5th level; [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) DC 28) to counteract the rune’s magic.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

**Force Blast** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=610), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) **Trigger** A non‑[undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=722) creature opens the door or critically fails an attempt to disarm or dispel the rune; **Effect** A 30‑foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) of force issues forth from the lock. Creatures within the cone must succeed a DC 29 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Fortitude saving throw or take 8d12 force damage. Those who fail the save are pushed 10 feet, and those who critically fail are pushed 20 feet and are [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=93) 2.

---
**Reset**
The rune resets 1 minute after it triggers.
name: Blast Tumbler

**Source** [Pathfinder #165: Eyes of Empty Death](/Sources.aspx?ID=81) pg. 52 **Complexity** Complex Seven nozzles hidden within holes in the 15‑foot-high ceiling release a poison gas. --- **Stealth** +21 (expert) or DC 31 (expert) to notice the hidden override mechanism on the north wall. **Disable** DC 33 [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) (master) to disable the hidden mechanism on the north wall, immediately sucking the gas from the room, or DC 29 Thievery (expert) to adequately plug one of the seven nozzles. When all seven nozzles are plugged or destroyed, the trap is deactivated. **AC** 31, **Fort** +24, **Ref** +18 **Nozzle Hardness** 20 **Nozzle HP** 32 (BT 16) **Immunities** [critical hits](/Rules.aspx?ID=2314), [object immunities](/Rules.aspx?ID=2161), precision damage **Gas Release** ([inhaled](/Traits.aspx?ID=634), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669)) **Trigger** Both secret doors are closed and at least one creature is in the room; **Effect** Gas fills the chamber. Creatures within the chamber must succeed on a DC 30 Fortitude save or become [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 1 (stupefied 2 on a critical failure). The trap then rolls initiative. --- **Routine** (1 action) The gas intensifies. Each creature in the room must make a DC 30 Fortitude save.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected and becomes temporarily immune to the gas for 1 hour, though if the creature has already been [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) by the trap, that condition remains for its normal duration.
**Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Failure** The creature becomes stupefied 1 for 24 hours. If the target is already stupefied, the condition value increases by 1 (to a maximum of stupefied 4) and the target takes 8d6 mental damage.
**Critical Effect** As failure, except the target takes double the mental damage. --- **Reset** After an hour, the trap deactivates; the gas disperses slowly, and the doors can be opened again. After 24 hours, the gas builds up and the trap can be triggered again.
name: Befuddling Gas Trap

<title level="1" right="Hazard 12" pfs="">[Images of Failure](/Hazards.aspx?ID=582)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Trap" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=158" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #165: Eyes of Empty Death](/Sources.aspx?ID=81) pg. 55

**Complexity** Simple

</column>

Psychically enhanced illusions flood the minds of creatures in the 40-foot-long, 15-foot-wide hallway (the white dotted box on the map) with memories of their past failures.

---

**Stealth**
DC 35 (expert) to realize the illusory images conceal a magical trap (noticing the images has a DC of 0)

**Disable**
DC 32 [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) (master) or [_dispel magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1493) (5th level; [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) DC 26) to weaken the images; three successes are required to disable the trap

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

**Echoes of Defeat** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=566), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) **Trigger** A creature ends a move action within the trap’s area; **Effect** The triggering creature takes 2d10+10 mental damage (DC 32 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Will save) as it recalls its past failures. A creature that takes damage hears a soft whisper offering, “Let me take something from you and I can stop the pain.” A creature who agrees loses access to a random skill feat that isn’t a prerequisite for another feat, and the creature doesn’t take further damage from Echoes of Defeat. This effect lasts for 1 week and can be ended by effects that remove curses.

---
**Reset**
The trap resets immediately and can affect the same creature multiple times on the creature’s turn.
name: Images of Failure

A creature that takes damage hears a soft whisper offering, “Let me take something from you and I can stop the pain.” A creature who agrees loses access to a random class feat that isn’t a prerequisite for another feat, and the creature doesn’t take further damage from Flood of Despair. **Source** [Gatewalkers (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=290) pg. 208 **Complexity** Simple A thin layer of canvas conceals this 10-foot‑square pit, the walls of which are studded with dagger-sized formian stingers. Creatures that fall into the trap risk being gouged by the poisonous barbs. --- **Stealth** DC 25 (trained) **Disable** DC 22 [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) (trained) **AC** 18, **Fort** +8, **Ref** +8 **Canvas HP** 30 (BT 15) **Immunities** [critical hits](/Rules.aspx?ID=2314), [object immunities](/Rules.aspx?ID=2161), precision damage **Entrench** ([attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=540)) **Trigger** A creature walks onto the canvas; **Effect** The creature falls into the concealed trench and takes falling damage (typically 10 bludgeoning damage). The creature is also gouged by three poisoned stingers as they fall. The creature can use the [Grab an Edge](/Actions.aspx?ID=2313) reaction to avoid falling.
**Melee** stinger +17, **Damage** 1d8+3 plus formian trench poison
**Formian Trench Poison** ([poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669)) **Saving Throw** Fortitude DC 21; **Maximum Duration** 6 rounds; **Stage 1** 1d6 poison damage (1 round); **Stage 2** 1d6 poison damage and [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) 1 (1 round); **Stage 3** 1d6 poison damage, clumsy 1, and [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) 1 (1 round); **Stage 4** 2d6 poison damage, clumsy 1, and enfeebled 1 (1 round) --- **Reset** Creatures can still fall into the trap, but the canvas must be reset manually for the trap to become [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=79) again.
name: Formian Sting Trench

<traits>

**Source** [Gatewalkers (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=290) pg. 211 **Complexity** Complex The spiritual imprints of long-dead explorers haunt this _aiudara_, manifesting as a trio of astral wraiths who guard the gateway jealously. --- **Stealth** +15 (expert) **Disable** DC 25 [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) (trained) to exorcise one of the wraiths or DC 25 [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) (expert) to erase one of the wraith’s astral sigils; three successful checks are required to disable the haunt **Forceful Screech** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) **Trigger** A creature comes within 30 feet of the aiudara; **Effect** Three aiudara wraiths screech in unison and manifest to protect the arch. Each creature within 30 feet must attempt a DC 27 Will save. The haunt rolls initiative.
**Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Failure** The creature takes 2d8+9 force damage.
**Critical Failure** The creature takes 4d8+9 force damage and is [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=73). --- **Routine** (3 actions; [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=710)) With spectral claws, the aiudara wraiths hurl trespassers through time and space. Each of the three wraiths has 1 action which it can use to make a forceful hand [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) against a creature within 60 feet. The haunt loses 1 action for each successful check to disable the haunt.
When the haunt rolls a critical hit to Strike a creature, the creature is instantly shunted to an empty space within 30 feet. The haunt chooses which space the creature is transported to.
**Melee** forceful hand +17 ([magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)), **Damage** 2d8+9 force; no multiple attack penalty --- **Reset** The aiudara wraiths disperse after 1 minute, but the haunt resets after 24 hours.
name: Haunted Aiudara

<title level="1" right="Hazard 5" pfs="">[Blooming Jijioa](/Hazards.aspx?ID=587)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Environmental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=591" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Gatewalkers (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=290) pg. 212

**Complexity** Simple

</column>

An invisible solar flare causes this patch of unassuming ground cover to suddenly bloom into a radioactive field.

---

**Stealth**
DC 26 (expert)

**Disable**
DC 22 [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) (trained) to remove a 5-foot square of jijioa, or 5 points of fire damage to destroy an affected area

<column gap="tiny">

**AC** 25, **Fort** +17, **Ref** +9

**HP**
52 (BT 26)

**Immunities**
[critical hits](/Rules.aspx?ID=2314), [object immunities](/Rules.aspx?ID=2161), precision damage

</column>

**Solar Bloom** <actions string="Reaction" /> **Trigger** The jijioa patch is exposed to a solar flare; **Effect** The countless jijioa flowers suddenly bloom, magnifying the ambient heat of the sun a hundredfold. Creatures in the jijioa patch take 4d6 fire damage (DC 22 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Fortitude save). Creatures that end their turn in the patch continue to take damage in this way as long as the jijioa is in bloom.

---
**Reset**
An hour after blooming, a jijioa patch goes dormant for 1 week.
name: Blooming Jijioa

<title level="1" right="Hazard 2" pfs="">[Cordon Alarm](/Hazards.aspx?ID=588)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
<trait label="Trap" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=715" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Gatewalkers (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=290) pg. 17

**Complexity** Simple

</column>

A hempen rope tied between trees emanates an invisible, cylindrical barrier that sounds an alarm when crossed.

---

**Stealth**
DC 18 (trained)

**Disable**
DC 18 [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) (trained) to move the rope without setting off the alarm, or [_dispel magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1493) (1st rank; [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) DC 16) to disable the rope’s magic

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

**Alarm** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) **Trigger** An object or creature of at least 1 Bulk crosses an invisible vertical barrier extending 30 feet above and below the rope; **Effect** The rope squawks like an angry squirrel from the point where the barrier was breached. This alarm is as loud as a human scream and lasts for 1 minute. If an item or creature is still breaching the barrier at the end of this duration, the alarm immediately triggers again.

---
**Reset**
The alarm resets immediately. Multiple breaches can cause the alarm to sound in multiple places at the same time, though the alarm can still be triggered only once per round.
name: Cordon Alarm

**Source** [Gatewalkers (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=290) pg. 20 **Complexity** Complex Cross-planar see page from the Oaksteward rebels’ ritual causes shadowy warping effects to strain and damage living creatures in the forest clearing. --- **Stealth** +7 (trained) to notice the air and plants wavering and shifting **Disable** DC 17 [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) (trained) to will the Universe’s hold on the area back into firmness and disable the effect for 1 hour, or [_dispel magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1493) (1st rank; [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) DC 15) to end the effect permanently **Shadowshift** ([primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) **Trigger** A character enters the affected area; **Effect** The hazard rolls initiative as the field of planar warping surges, filling the forest clearing. --- **Routine** (1 action) Each creature in the clearing must attempt a DC 17 Fortitude save as the planar warping threatens to create microscopic tears inside their body. [Fey](/Traits.aspx?ID=599), [plant](/Traits.aspx?ID=668) creatures, and [druids](/Classes.aspx?ID=34) are unaffected.
**Critical Success** As success, and the creature is healed by the primal magic, regaining 1d6 Hit Points.
**Success** The creature is unaffected and immune to the effect for 24 hours.
**Failure** The creature takes 1d6+3 force damage.
**Critical Failure** The creature takes 2d6+6 force damage. --- **Reset** This effect continues for 1 day, after which it ends and cannot be triggered again without another ritual.
name: Shadowshift Field

**Source** [Gatewalkers (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=290) pg. 28 **Complexity** Complex Pipes extending from a pressurized water tank spray flesh-cutting jets of water at unwary passersby. --- **Stealth** +11 (trained); DC 19 (expert) to notice the water tank bulging under the immense pressure **Disable** DC 18 [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) (trained) to puncture a pipe and release the pressure safely, or DC 16 [Engineering Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) (trained) to find and turn a pressure release valve; any combination of three punctured pipes or turned valves disables the trap **AC** 18, **Fort** +11, **Ref** +3 **Tank Hardness** 7 **Tank HP** 50 (BT 25);**Pipe Hardness** 7, **Pipe HP** 30 (BT 15) **Immunities** [critical hits](/Rules.aspx?ID=2190), [object immunities](/Rules.aspx?ID=2161), precision damage **Water Jets** **Trigger** A creature steps onto a floor space in this room; **Effect** Seals on the water pipes burst, issuing forth high-pressure water jets in random directions. The hazard makes a water jet Strike against the triggering creature. The hazard rolls initiative.
**Violent Deluge** **Trigger** The water tank is [broken](/Conditions.aspx?ID=60) or [destroyed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=46); **Effect** A torrent of water bursts from the shattered tank, dealing 3d8 bludgeoning damage to each creature within 15 feet ([basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) DC 22 Reflex save). The trap is then disabled. --- **Routine** (3 actions) For each pipe destroyed or disabled, the trap loses 1 action. On each of the trap’s actions, the trap targets a random creature in the room with a water jet Strike.
**Ranged** water jet +11 ([water](/Traits.aspx?ID=732)), **Damage** 2d8 piercing; no multiple attack penalty --- **Reset** The trap deactivates after 5 rounds. It cannot be reactivated until the reservoir is refilled, which takes an average of 5 days.
name: Reservoir Trap

**Source** [Gatewalkers (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=290) pg. 29 **Complexity** Simple The frayed ropes on both sides of this bridge snap when enough weight is put on the bridge’s center. --- **Stealth** DC 23 (trained) **Disable** DC 20 [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) (trained) to reinforce and secure the ropes on one side of the bridge **AC** 10, **Fort** +1, **Ref** +1 **Rope HP** 6 (BT 3) **Immunities** [critical hits](/Rules.aspx?ID=2190), [object immunities](/Rules.aspx?ID=2161), precision damage **Collapse** **Trigger** A Medium or larger creature steps onto the middle of the bridge (any of the squares marked “T” on the map); **Effect** The ropes on both sides of the bridge snap simultaneously, causing the bridge to collapse. Any creature on the bridge falls to the forest floor 50 feet below and takes 25 bludgeoning damage. A creature within 10 feet of one end of the bridge can attempt a DC 18 Reflex saving throw to Grab an Edge along the side of either **A8** or **A13**.
If one side of the bridge has been disabled by resecuring the ropes, a character on any section of the bridge can attempt a DC 18 Reflex save to hang onto the bridge as one side of it falls. The dangling bridge thereafter functions as a ladder connecting either area **A8** or **A13** (whichever was secured) to the forest floor.
name: Falling Bridge

The DC to [Climb](/Actions.aspx?ID=2374) out of the pit is 10. --- **Stealth** DC 21 (or 0 if the trap door is [disabled](/Actions.aspx?ID=2411) or [broken](/Conditions.aspx?ID=60)) **Disable** DC 18 [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) (trained) to jam the trap door open **AC** 18, **Fort** +7, **Ref** +7 **Trap door Hardness** 9 **Trap door HP** 30 **Immunities** [critical hits](/Rules.aspx?ID=2190), [object immunities](/Rules.aspx?ID=2161), precision damage **Dispose** **Trigger** A creature steps onto the trap door (marked “T” on the map); **Effect** The triggering creature falls into the pit. The creature can use the [Grab an Edge](/Actions.aspx?ID=2313) reaction to avoid falling. Failing that, the creature falls 20 feet (taking 10 bludgeoning damage from the fall) and takes 3d6 piercing damage from the metal spikes jutting up from the pit’s floor. A creature who takes damage from the spikes is exposed to tetanus.
**Tetanus** ([disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578)) **Saving Throw** DC 14 Fortitude; **Onset** 10 days; **Stage 1** [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) 1 (1 week); **Stage 2** clumsy 2 and can’t speak (1 day); **Stage 3** [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85) with spasms (1 day); **Stage 4** death

<title level="1" right="Hazard 4" pfs="">[Scholar's Bane](/Hazards.aspx?ID=598)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Complex" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=30" />
<trait label="Illusion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=629" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
<trait label="Trap" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=715" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Gatewalkers (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=290) pg. 55

**Complexity** Complex

</column>

Illusions overwhelm the minds of particularly intelligent creatures, forcing them to relive every intellectual failure and shame they’ve ever suffered.

---

**Stealth**
+14 (trained) to notice magical sensors hidden within four of the carvings’ eyes

**Disable**
DC 22 [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) (trained) to carve out one eye sensor, DC 22 [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) or [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) (trained) to blank out one’s mind (disables trap for self only), or [_dispel magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1493) (2nd rank; [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) DC 20) to counteract one eye sensor

<column gap="tiny">

**AC** 20, **Fort** +12, **Ref** +8

**Eye Hardness**
6

**Eye HP**
20

</column>

**Reveal Failure** <actions string="Reaction" /> **Trigger** A creature with an Intelligence score of 10 or greater enters the room; **Effect** The trap fills the triggering creature’s mind with shameful memories. The creature must succeed at a DC 22 Will save or take 2d8+2 mental damage. The trap then rolls initiative.

---
**Routine**
(4 actions) The trap uses each action to target a creature in the room with an Intelligence score of 10 or greater. A target must succeed at a DC 22 Will save or take 2d8+2 mental damage. On a critical failure, the creature takes a –10-foot status penalty to Speed for 1 minute as they stagger under the weight of their failures. The trap loses 1 action for each eye sensor disabled or destroyed.

---
**Reset**
The trap can function for 10 rounds, which don’t need to be consecutive, before it must recharge for 24 hours.
name: Scholar's Bane

Sounds become muted in a 20-foot-wide, 100-foot-long segment of this street corridor.

---

**Stealth**
DC 25 (expert)

**Disable**
DC 25 [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) (expert) to twist the magic of the ley line around you, disabling the hazard for only you; or [_dispel magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1493) (4th rank; [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) DC 23) to disable the hazard permanently

</column>

A silenced creature makes no sound and can’t use abilities with the [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=697) or [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541) traits, including casting spells that don’t have the [subtle](/Traits.aspx?ID=522) trait.

<title level="1" right="Hazard 5" pfs="">[Memory of Osoyo](/Hazards.aspx?ID=603)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=723" />
<trait label="Complex" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=30" />
<trait label="Environmental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=591" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Gatewalkers (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=290) pg. 100

**Complexity** Complex

</column>

The party’s memories of Osoyo coalesce into a titanic mirage in the storm clouds above.

---

**Stealth**
+12 (expert)

**Disable**
DC 22 [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) (trained) or DC 24 [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) (trained) to divert the memory’s rage; three total successful checks are required to disable the hazard

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

**Glacial Gust** <actions string="Reaction" /> **Trigger** The characters’ shared vision during **Event 15** subsides; **Effect** The memory of Osoyo unleashes a tremendous sigh of freezing wind, coating the deck of the Mermaid’s Tears in ice in a 60-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386). (The hazard can choose any point for the cone to originate.) The area becomes [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2366). Creatures in the area must succeed at a DC 26 Fortitude save or take 1d6 [persistent cold damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) as the freezing wind cuts to the bone. The hazard then rolls initiative.

---
**Routine**
(1 action) The memory of Osoyo gives a keening scream that shatters the layer of ice coating the deck of the Mermaid’s Tears. Any creature on [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2366) created by the memory’s Glacial Gust takes 2d8+7 cold damage (DC 26 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save) from the icy shrapnel.
name: Memory of Osoyo

<traits>
<trait label="Mechanical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=646" />
<trait label="Trap" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=715" />

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Gatewalkers (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=290) pg. 115

**Complexity** Simple

</column>

The ground here is made of loosely packed soil that, when stepped upon, collapses.

---

**Stealth**
DC 25 (expert)

**Disable**
DC 22 [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) (trained) to reinforce the dirt so it’s safe to walk on, or DC 18 [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) (trained) to collapse the trap from a safe distance

<column gap="tiny">

**AC** 10, **Fort** +2, **Ref** +3

**Hardness**
1

**HP**
6 (BT 3)

**Immunities**
[critical hits](/Rules.aspx?ID=2314), [object immunities](/Rules.aspx?ID=2161), precision damage

</column>

**Collapse** <actions string="Reaction" /> **Trigger** A creature steps onto any space in the dotted area on the map, or at least 30 pounds of pressure is put on the area; **Effect** The ground gives way. The triggering creature falls 20 feet down the hole, taking 10 bludgeoning damage and landing [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88) in area **E10**. A creature can [Grab an Edge](/Actions.aspx?ID=2313) to avoid falling.

---
**Reset**
Creatures can still fall into the pit, but the soil must be replaced for the trap to become [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=79) again.
name: Valmar's Pit Trap

**Source** [Gatewalkers (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=290) pg. 116 **Complexity** Complex The white brick floor radiates ice-cold air. --- **Stealth** +16 (expert) or DC 31 (master) to notice the cold fog rising from the floor **Disable** DC 24 [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) (expert) to erase a rune, or [_dispel magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1493) (3rd rank; [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) DC 24) to dispel a rune; all three runes must be erased or dispelled to disable the trap. **AC** 27, **Fort** +13, **Ref** +17 **Hardness** 14 **HP** 56 (BT 28) to destroy the bricks and disable the trap **Immunities** [critical hits](/Rules.aspx?ID=2314), [object immunities](/Rules.aspx?ID=2161), precision damage **Cold Snap** **Trigger** A creature steps on the white bricks; **Effect** The trap makes a frost jet Strike against the triggering creature and rolls initiative. --- **Routine** (3 actions) With each action, the trap attempts a frost jet Strike against any creature standing on the white bricks. The trap loses 1 action per disabled or dispelled rune.
**Ranged** frost jet +20, **Damage** 2d6 cold plus 1d4 [persistent cold](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) and frostbite; no multiple attack penalty
**Frostbite** A creature that takes persistent cold damage from the trap’s frost jet Strike must attempt a DC 18 Fortitude save.
**Critical Success** The creature isn’t affected and is immune to this frostbite effect for 24 hours.
**Success** The creature isn’t affected.
**Failure** The creature can’t stop shivering and becomes [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) 1 while the persistent damage lasts.
**Critical Failure** As failure, and the creature becomes [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=73) while the persistent damage lasts. --- **Reset** The trap deactivates and resets after 1 minute
name: Freezing Floor

markdown:
**Source** [Gatewalkers (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=290) pg. 124 **Complexity** Simple This stone bookshelf is badly compromised. It’s just one push away from toppling over. --- **Stealth** DC 18 (trained) to notice the shelf’s precarious instability **Disable** DC 22 [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) (expert) or [Engineering Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) (trained) to shore up the shelf **AC** 18, **Fort** +8, **Ref** +8 **Hardness** 8 **HP** 30 (BT 15), any attack that fails to destroy the shelf triggers its Topple reaction **Immunities** [critical hits](/Rules.aspx?ID=2314), [object immunities](/Rules.aspx?ID=2161), precision damage **Topple** **Trigger** The shelf is attacked or touched forcefully (such as by a creature [Shoved](/Actions.aspx?ID=2380) into it); **Effect** The shelf tumbles over in the direction opposite its triggering creature, crashing onto the ground in a 10-foot-by-10-foot area. Creatures in the area take 4d8+10 bludgeoning damage (DC 25 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). A creature that critically fails its save falls [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88). The affected area becomes [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2366).
If the toppling shelf crashes into a space occupied by another toppling shelf, it triggers that shelf’s Topple reaction. This can result in a chain reaction that causes multiple shelves to Topple, one after the other.
name: Toppling Shelves

**Source** [Gatewalkers (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=290) pg. 129 **Complexity** Complex An incorporeal Sarkorian deity manifests before the party and challenges their resolve. Only aspirants whose motives are pure and whose hearts are in tune with the natural world can hope to overcome this “test of the god caller.” --- **Stealth** +12 (expert) **Disable** Three DC 27 [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) or [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) (trained) checks can disable the haunt **Manifest** **Trigger** Sakuachi makes an offering at the well shrine; **Effect** The Sarkorian deity inhabiting the well surges to life, manifesting as a large, ghostly beast surrounded by throngs of smaller animals. The rush of spiritual energy washes over everyone in the well, dealing 8d6 force damage (DC 25 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Will save). --- **Routine** (3 actions) The Sarkorian divinity commands a swarm of ghostly animals to overwhelm creatures in a 10-foot [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385) in the well. The haunt can select up to three different points to target, spending 1 action per burst. Creatures in an affected area take 4d6 force damage and must attempt a DC 25 Will save. The haunt loses 1 action for each successful check to disable the haunt.
**Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target takes half damage.
**Failure** The target takes full damage and is [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) until the beginning of the haunt’s next turn.
**Critical Failure** The target takes double damage, is off-guard until the beginning of the haunt’s next turn, and is [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) 1 for 1 round. --- **Reset** 8 hours
name: Test of the God Caller

**Source** [Gatewalkers (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=290) pg. 145 **Complexity** Simple Several hidden nozzles spray colorless sleeping gas into the seating area. --- **Stealth** DC 28 (expert) **Disable** DC 28 [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) (expert) to crimp shut the primary feeding tube for the gas, or DC 31 [Perception](/Rules.aspx?ID=2040) (expert) to discover the hidden bypass switch next to the door to area **A3** that shuts the trap off **Gas Spray** ([incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696)) **Trigger** Pressure is applied to any of the steps in the middle of the seating area, or applied to the manual trigger on the stage. This manual trigger can activate the trap even if its hidden bypass switch is engaged; **Effect** Sleeping gas is silently sprayed into the seating area. All creatures in the area must roll a DC 26 Will save to resist its effects. The gas persists in the area for 3 rounds before becoming inert. Any creature who ends their turn in this area during these 3 rounds must attempt a new DC 26 Will save.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected and realizes that the area has been gassed.
**Success** The creature is unaffected but doesn’t notice the gas.
**Failure** The creature feels a bit drowsy and lethargic, and becomes [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=73) for 1 minute. If the creature is already fatigued, it instead falls [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95). If it’s still unconscious after 1 minute, it wakes up automatically.
**Critical Failure** The creature falls unconscious. If it’s still unconscious after 1 hour, it wakes up automatically.
name: Soporific Lecture

markdown:
**Source** [Gatewalkers (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=290) pg. 152 **Complexity** Complex The incense burners begin to smoke, filling the air with strangely nostalgic scents. --- **Stealth** +30 (master) **Disable** DC 26 [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) (expert) to enter a state akin to lucid dreaming so as to unweave the nightmare from within, or DC 30 [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) (master) to stand resolute against the nightmares and turn the fear back upon itself **Light Incense Burners** ([incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696)) **Trigger** A creature lies down on the bed, or a living creature spends more than 3 rounds inside this room; **Effect** Incense swiftly fills the room (even if the incense burners have been removed or were destroyed). All creatures in area **A12** must attempt a DC 32 Fortitude save. The haunt then rolls initiative.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) 1 by the incense’s scent.
**Failure** The creature falls [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95). If it’s still unconscious after 1 minute, it wakes up automatically.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but if it’s still unconscious after 1 hour, it wakes up automatically. --- **Routine** (2 actions; [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) The haunt uses its first action to light incense burners again, forcing any creature that isn’t already [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95) to save against that effect once more. Its second action is to cause any unconscious creatures in the room to experience horrific, vivid dreams about being lost in the arctic during a blizzard, while enormous furred figures—saumen kar— lunge at them through the snow to attack repeatedly. Each unconscious creature must attempt a DC 28 Fortitude saving throw. If at the end of the round there are no unconscious creatures in the room, Etward’s nightmare ends and the trap deactivates.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected and wakes up.
**Success** The creature remains unconscious and takes 1d10 cold damage and 1d10 mental damage. This damage does not wake the creature, and those who are awake can see the unconscious creature thrash as if in the throes of a nightmare while their body rimes over with layers of frost. The creature can fight against the bitter cold and monstrous shapes by attempting a DC 28 Will save as a three-action activity on its turn— on a success, the creature wakes up.
**Failure** As success, but 1d10+6 cold and 1d10+6 mental damage, and with a DC 32 Will save to wake up.
**Critical Failure** The creature remains unconscious and takes 2d10+12 cold damage and 2d10+12 mental damage. --- **Reset** The hazard resets when Etward dreams in this room.
name: Etward's Nightmare

markdown:
**Source** [Pathfinder Adventure Path #219: Lord of the Trinity Star](/Sources.aspx?ID=283) pg. 20 **Complexity** Complex The rundown furnaces blaze to life, filling with metal and fire, while the wall of hardened lava to the east appears to revert to red hot molten rock. --- **Stealth** 28 **Disable** DC 38 [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) (master) to exorcise the spirits or DC 41 [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) to quell them with furious anger; three total successes are required to disable the haunt **AC** 36, **Fort** +30, **Ref** +22 **Forge Hardness** 24 **HP** 94 (BT 47) **Immunities** critical hits, fire, object immunities, precision damage; **Weaknesses** cold 10 **Searing Heat** (divine, fire) **Trigger** A creature comes within 30 feet of either forge; **Effect** The eastern wall appears to transform into molten lava, and dozens of shrieking human spirits made of fire fly out of the lava to spiral and scorch through the room. Creatures in area must attempt a DC 34 Reflex save.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature takes 4d6 fire damage
**Failure** The creature takes 8d6 fire damage, 1d6 persistent fire damage, and is [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 1.
**Critical Failure** The creature takes 16d6 fire damage, 2d6 persistent fire damage, and is frightened 2. --- **Routine** (4 actions) The burning spirits shriek and howl, swooping in and out of the forges to the south, which roar into scorching life. The spirits scoop up gobs of phantasmal molten dawnsilver and hurl them at different targets in the room. Up to four randomly determined creatures are targeted in this way with molten dawnsilver Strikes (the spirits can target any creature in the area). A single creature may be targeted only once per round, so if there are fewer than four different targets, the haunt’s extra actions are lost. The haunt also loses two actions from its routine if one of the two forges are destroyed. On a critical hit from a molten dawnsilver Strike, the target is [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) 1 for 1 round as the molten dawnsilver swiftly cools and encases the target. Molten dawnsilver produced by the haunt vanishes entirely 1 round after it’s been thrown. **Ranged** molten dawnsilver +29, **Damage** 3d10+11 bludgeoning plus 2d6 fire --- **Reset** 1 hour
name: Haunted Forge

**Source** [Pathfinder Adventure Path #219: Lord of the Trinity Star](/Sources.aspx?ID=283) pg. 20 **Complexity** Complex A mass of illusions floods the senses of creatures, plunging them into an ever-shifting maze of fluid realities. --- **Stealth** 31 **Disable** DC 38 [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) (legendary) to seize strands of a false reality and tug at them to unravel the surrounding illusions, DC 41 [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) to take command of the illusions in a false reality and force them to consume themselves, [_dispel magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1493) (7th-rank; counteract DC 39) to counteract one of the four realities the illusion creates **AC** 39, **Fort** +28, **Ref** +28 **Reality Hardness** 20 **HP** 90 **Immunities** critical hits, object immunities, precision damage, plus one type of damage defined by the current reality **Reality Undone** (illusion) **Trigger** A non-follower of Xanderghul attempts to enter the hallway or chambers to the east of this area; **Effect** A flood of illusions overwhelms in a rush of images and sounds, engulfing the triggering creature and all of their allies within the dawnsilver workshop. Affected creatures must succeed at a DC 34 Will save or become [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 1 (stupefied 2 on a critical failure) for 24 hours. --- **Routine** (1 action) The trap shifts from the current reality into a randomly determined one that affects all those lost within the illusion in a different way. The new reality restores 20 Hit Points to itself if it had previously taken damage. Each time a PC succeeds at a check or dispel magic attempt to disable the trap or the current reality is reduced to 0 HP, the current illusory reality vanishes, leaving the PCs in an infinite haze of nothingness save for a three-tailed comet burning in the “sky” above (recognizable as the Trinity Star with a DC 25 Arcana, Nature, Occultism, or Religion check) until the trap takes its next action and randomly determines its new reality from the options that remain. If only one endless reality remains when it’s disabled or destroyed, the PCs are returned to the real world and the trap is entirely disabled. When the endless reality shifts, determine its effects and appearance by randomly selecting from the following (rerolling any results that have already been disabled or destroyed). Each time a PC critically succeeds at a saving throw to resist a reality’s damage, the trap degrades; it’s disable DCs and hardness are reduced by 2 with each such critical success. If the trap’s hardness is reduced to 0 in this way, the entire trap is disabled at once.
**Burning Reality** (fire) **Additional Immunity** fire; **Effect** The world appears to catch on fire, while legions of burning monsters attack the party. Every PC takes 10d6 fire damage (DC 34 basic Reflex save).
**Frozen Reality** (cold) **Additional Immunity** cold; **Effect** The world appears to crust over with ice, while armies of frozen ghosts assault the PCs. Every PC takes 10d6 cold damage (DC 34 basic Reflex save).
**Impossible Reality** (mental) **Additional Immunity** physical; **Effect** The world distorts into a reality of impossible geometry as the PCs feel their own bodies distort to match twisted reflections of unworkable configurations. Every PC takes 10d6 mental damage (DC 34 basic Will save).
**Melting Reality** (acid) **Additional Immunity** acid; **Effect** The world appears to dissolve away, as slithering oozes swarm out of the surroundings to engulf the PCs. Every PC takes 10d6 acid damage (DC 34 basic Fortitude save).
name: Endless Realities

markdown:
**Source** [Pathfinder Adventure Path #219: Lord of the Trinity Star](/Sources.aspx?ID=283) pg. 50 **Complexity** Complex The shaft walls vanish and are replaced by an endless field of stars in all direction, as if the viewer were suddenly lost in outer space. --- **Stealth** 30 **Disable** DC 40 [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) (master) to unweave the magic left in the wake of the Trinity Star’s passage, DC 43 [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) (expert) to etch temporary runes to siphon away the magic, or [_dispel magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1493) (8th rank; counteract DC 33) to counteract the magic; three successes are required to disable the trap. Once the PCs are within the trap’s overwhelming illusion, they can still attempt these checks to disable it, but the check DCs increase by 2. **Moment of Glory** (illusion, mental) **Trigger** A creature moves (or falls) more than 60 feet down the shaft below the illusory throne; **Effect** All creatures in the shaft suddenly witness an endless night sky all around them, as if they were hurtling through outer space with no sign of any nearby planetary bodies. Each affected creature must attempt a DC 40 Will save to resist being [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 1 (or stupefied 2 on a critical failure) for 24 hours as the visual overwhelms them. The trap then rolls initiative. --- **Routine** **A Voyage Through the Infinite** (1 action) Every round on its turn, the trap inflicts all creatures in the shaft with the perception that they have been hurtling at an unimaginable speed through the vastness of space. The stars are so distant that no indication of movement can be observed or felt beyond that overwhelming mental conviction. At the same time, the creatures feel as if something unimaginably powerful and hungry is chasing them... and gaining on them. Any PC who looks over their shoulder sees that the stars are indeed winking out behind them, as if an immense black object were growing closer or as if the stars themselves were vanishing. While the PC’s body is somehow protected by the vastness of space, their minds are not—each PC must attempt a DC 40 Will save. **Critical Success** As success below, but the creature also realizes they’re reliving the memories of Runelord Xanderghul—like him, they’re escaping some sort of horrific fate and are hurtling toward Golarion. The next time this creature achieves a critical success against this trap, they suddenly see Golarion loom large, and as they witness their “crash” into the Throne of the Troll King, they suddenly wake, prone, in area C1 at the heart Xin-Cyrusian. If any other PCs are still trapped in the Falling Star’s Memories, a PC in Xin-Cyrusian knows instinctively that they can call out to those trapped PCs—each PC in Xin-Cyrusian who does so reduces the save DC against A Voyage Through the Infinite for those still trapped by 5.
**Success** The creature realizes they’re experiencing the path the Trinity Star took through the cosmos and becomes [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 1.
**Failure** The creature’s grip on reality fades as they experience what seems like years or even decades of hurtling adrift through a vast expanse. The creature becomes [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 1 for 24 hours or increases the value of their stupefied condition by 1 if they’re already stupefied.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but stupefied 2 (or increase the stupefied condition by 2). **Special** If this trap would cause a creature’s stupefied condition to exceed stupefied 5, the creature instead “crashes” to the ground on the hilltop outside, about ten feet from the Throne of the Troll King. The creature takes 10d6 bludgeoning damage (DC 40 basic Reflex save), and their stupefied condition now has an unlimited duration. --- **Reset** The trap resets automatically
name: A Falling Star's Memories

<title level="1" right="Hazard 16" pfs="">[Mirror of Glory](/Hazards.aspx?ID=623)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=723" />
<trait label="Complex" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=30" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
<trait label="Trap" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=715" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder Adventure Path #219: Lord of the Trinity Star](/Sources.aspx?ID=283) pg. 57

**Complexity** Complex

</column>

A reflective field of energy fills a large archway in a gatehouse, in which viewers regard themselves dressed in fine clothes, bowing down before Runelord Xanderghul.

---

**Stealth**
34

**Disable**
DC 37 [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) (master) to disrupt one of the six anchor points that link the [_paradigm cube_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4041) to the mirror, DC 34 [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) (legendary) to use knowledge of magic that opposes pride to unravel the mirror’s power, DC 37 (master) [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) to use one’s force of personality to stand against the arrogance amplified by the mirror, or [_dispel magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1493) (8th-rank; counteract DC 36) to counteract one of the six faces of the _paradigm cube_; six successes are required to disable the Mirror of Glory

<column gap="tiny">

**AC** 39, **Fort** +30, **Ref** +33

**Archway Hardness**
27

**HP**
106 (BT 53)

**Immunities**
critical hits, object immunities, precision damage

</column>

**Bow Before Your God** <actions string="Reaction" /> **Trigger** A creature touches the mirror or Seeks the reflection while being adjacent to the mirror; **Effect** The image of Xanderghul that appears behind the reflected PCs spreads his arms wide in glory, transforming the world presented in the reflection. Each PC reflected in the mirror (regardless of whether they’re looking at the reflection or not) must attempt a DC 37 Will save to avoid being compelled to drop to their knees before Xanderghul’s glory—on a failure, the PC becomes [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88), and the feelings of inferiority cause them to become [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 1 for 24 hours. The trap then rolls initiative.

---
**Routine**
(6 actions) On its turn, the Mirror of Glory targets random PCs in the area with distortions of their reality, spending an action to target one PC. If it has actions remaining after targeting the PCs, it spends any remaining actions repairing damage done to itself at the rate of 25 HP restored per action spent. If the mirror hasn’t been damaged, then these unused actions are lost. Each time the Mirror of Glory targets a PC with a distortion, roll 1d6 and consult the entries below to determine what the PC experiences and any subsequent effects. The trap loses one action for each successful check made to disable it, but this doesn’t reduce the number of distortions it’s capable of creating. When a PC experiences a distortion, they take 10d6 mental damage (DC 37 basic Will save), but on a failed saving throw, the PC suffers that distortion’s additional effect.

**1—Beauty:** The PC regards Xanderghul as the most attractive figure they’ve ever seen. **Additional effect:** [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) 1 for 1 round.

**2—Cruelty:** The PC sees Xanderghul as a demonic figure capable of unimaginable cruelty, who lashes out at the PC with dozens of blades and poisoned needles meant to carve away their strength. **Additional effect:** [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) 1 for 24 hours.

**3—Disappointment:** To the PC, Xanderghul suddenly seems to be an intimidating parental figure, commanding officer, or other person with authority whose disapproval induces tremors of nervousness. **Additional effect:** [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) 1 for 24 hours.

**4—Jealousy:** The PC watches as a glorious plume of peacock tail feathers spreads behind Xanderghul, filling them with jealousy and potentially driving them to lash out unpredictably against their allies in hopes of becoming the only one worthy of Xanderghul’s attention. **Additional effect:** [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=63) for 1 round. 

**5—Despair:** The PC feels their own flesh rotting away as they realize the Xanderghul who stands before them exists outside of the laws of time. **Additional effect:** [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) 1.

**6—Worship:** The PC is filled with the conviction that Xanderghul is the one true god and feels the urge to pray to him for absolution. **Additional effect:** [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=93) 3.

---
**Reset**
The trap deactivates, then resets instantly.
name: Mirror of Glory

**Source** [Pathfinder Adventure Path #219: Lord of the Trinity Star](/Sources.aspx?ID=283) pg. 60 **Complexity** Complex Plumes of magenta and violet light and vapor shift and writhe out of a hole in reality surrounded by the fragments of a _runewell_ suspended mid-explosion. --- **Stealth** 27 **Disable** DC 37 [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) (master) or DC 40 [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) (master) to redirect the magic back in on itself and cancel out the danger, or DC 40 [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) (legendary) to reposition _runewell_ fragments so they’re not dangerous; three total successes are required to disable the shattered _runewell_ **AC** 34, **Fort** +29, **Ref** +26 **Shattered Runewell Hardness** 23 **HP** 90 (BT 45) **Immunities** critical hits, object immunities, precision damage **Frightful Shriek** (emotion, fear, mental) **Trigger** Intruders enter the Eye of Arrogance; **Effect** All of the illusory peacocks in the demiplane emit loud shrieks of anger. All intruders to the Eye of Arrogance must attempt a DC 37 Will save or become [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 1 (frightened 2 on a critical failure). The shattered _runewell_ then rolls initiative. --- **Routine** (3 actions) When the shattered _runewell_ takes its f irst action, a towering, 30-foot-tall image of Runelord Xanderghul formed from scintillating magenta and violet light and fire looms up from the runewell. On each of the hazard’s three actions, this manifestation of Runelord Xanderghul scowls down at the PCs, and tendrils of strangely colored fire lash out of the runewell, each targeting a different PC in the area. The effects of these tendrils vary according to their color; these ranged Strikes can target anyone in the Eye of Arrogance and accrue multiple attack penalties normally. **Ranged** iridescent fire +27 (mental), Damage 3d10+6 mental damage (plus the struck creature is [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) 1 for 1 round if they fail a DC 37 Will save)
**Ranged** black fire +27 (void), Damage 3d10+6 void damage plus 2d6 persistent acid damage
**Ranged** crimson fire +27 (agile), Damage 3d10+6 slashing damage plus 2d6 persistent bleed
name: Shattered Runewell of Pride

**Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 306 **Complexity** Simple A sportlebore hides amid victuals on a plate of food or within a pack of trail rations. --- **Stealth** DC 26 (expert) **Disable** A creature that notices the sportlebore can remove it from the food it’s [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=79) within automatically as an [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) action. **Mimic Food** **Trigger** A creature eats food the sportlebore is [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=79) within; **Effect** The sportlebore rapidly multiplies in the host’s stomach, exposing the host to sportlebore infestation.
**Sportlebore Infestation** ([disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578)) **Saving Throw** DC 22 Fortitude; **Stage 1** carrier with no ill effect (1 day); **Stage 2** [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) 1 (1 hour); **Stage 3** enfeebled 2 (1 hour); **Stage 4** 4d6 bludgeoning damage (DC 25 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Fortitude save) as the host painfully vomits out a sportlebore swarm and returns to stage 1
name: Sportlebore

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Dark Archives (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=293) pg. 122

</column>

The reflection of a wall shows a phantom door where none exists. This door can only be manipulated while viewed through the mirror's reflection.

---

**Stealth**
DC 15

**Disable**
DC 18 [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) to open the door while viewing the reflection

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

**Appear** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=629)) **Trigger** A creature views the wall through a mirror's reflection; **Effect** A door appears in the mirror's reflection of the wall. The triggering creature can open and manipulate this door only as long as they view it through the mirror and only by succeeding at a DC 18 Thievery check to disable the trap.

---
**Reset**
automatic
name: Mirror Door

<title level="1" right="Hazard -1" pfs="">[Shuffling Hall](/Hazards.aspx?ID=627)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Mechanical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=646" />
<trait label="Trap" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=715" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Dark Archives (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=293) pg. 122

**Complexity** Simple

</column>

Four pressure plates along this mirrored hall rotate vertically when stepped on, sending a creature on the pressure plate tipping into an adjacent featureless room while simultaneously replacing the pressure plate and walls in the hall. Each pressure plate dumps creatures into a different room.

---

**Stealth**
DC 15

**Disable**
DC 12 [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) to deactivate one pressure plate

<column gap="tiny">

**AC** 18, **Fort** +2, **Ref** +5

**Hardness**
4

**HP**
12 (BT 6)

**Immunities**
object immunities

</column>

**Flip** <actions string="Reaction" /> **Trigger** A creature steps on a pressure plate; **Effect** The floor and adjoining wall rotate up and over on a central axle, tipping the triggering creature into an adjacent featureless room and simultaneously replacing the pressure plate and wall with another set on the same axle. The triggering creature is pushed into an adjoining room, takes 1d8 bludgeoning damage, and lands [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88). A creature who succeeds at a DC 19 Reflex save takes no damage and rolls out of the way of the trap, returning to the space they were in before stepping on the pressure plate.

---
**Reset**
automatic
name: Shuffling Hall

<title level="1" right="Hazard 0" pfs="">[Confounding Portal](/Hazards.aspx?ID=628)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Illusion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=629" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
<trait label="Mental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=647" />
<trait label="Trap" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=715" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Dark Archives (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=293) pg. 122

**Complexity** Simple

</column>

Tiny runes carved around a doorframe or on a hall's walls surreptitiously confound creatures into circling the room or hallway they attempted to exit or pass through.

---

**Stealth**
DC 16

**Disable**
DC 17 [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) to scratch out the runes, or DC 16 [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) to destroy the runes

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

**Confounding Misdirection** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=629), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) **Trigger** A creature passes through the rune-carved doorway or hallway; **Effect** The creature is confused by the illusions and directed back into the room they attempted to exit through another doorway or back to the hallway's beginning. This feels no different to the triggering creature than walking through the doorway or hallway, and onlookers are similarly befuddled. A creature who succeeds at a DC 19 Will save resists this illusion and exits the room or proceeds down the hall without being misdirected.

---
**Reset**
automatic
name: Confounding Portal

**Source** [Dark Archives (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=293) pg. 122 **Complexity** Simple Illusions cloaking this chamber cause it to appear distorted and constantly shifting, with the room and all its contents appearing tilted, irregular, and in motion. --- **Stealth** DC 20 **Disable** DC 17 [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) (trained) to calculate the room's dimensions despite the illusions, or [_dispel magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1493) (1st level; counteract DC 17) to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=3280) the illusions **Disorient** ([arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=629), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) **Trigger** A creature enters the room; **Effect** Illusions cause the appearance of the room and the objects inside it to constantly warp, shift, bend, and distort for 1 minute. Each creature in the room while it's distorting must attempt a DC 17 Perception check. **Critical Success** The creature disbelieves the illusions and is unaffected.
**Success** The creature disbelieves the illusions but still sees them. It treats the room as difficult terrain.
**Failure** The creature is fooled by the illusions. It treats the room as difficult terrain and becomes [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) 1. As long as it remains in the room, it can't reduce its sickened value below 1.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but each time the creature attempts a move action, it must succeed at a DC 5 flat check or they lose the action, and on a critical failure, it also falls [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88). **Critical Success** The creature disbelieves the illusions and is unaffected.
**Success** The creature disbelieves the illusions but still sees them. It treats the room as difficult terrain.
**Failure** The creature is fooled by the illusions. It treats the room as difficult terrain and becomes [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34). As long as it remains in the room, it can't reduce its sickened value below 1.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but each time the creature attempts a move action, it must succeed at a DC 5 flat check or they lose the action, and on a critical failure, it also falls [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31). --- **Reset** 1 minute
name: Disorienting Illusions

<title level="1" right="Hazard 1" pfs="">[Reflected Desires](/Hazards.aspx?ID=630)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Enchantment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=61" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
<trait label="Mental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=647" />
<trait label="Trap" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=715" />
<trait label="Visual" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=727" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Dark Archives (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=293) pg. 123

**Complexity** Simple

</column>

This mirror reflects the viewer's deepest desires.

---

**Stealth**
DC 17 (0 to notice the mirror)

**Disable**
DC 18 [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) (trained) to shroud the mirror without looking at it, or DC 17 [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) (trained) to erect mental barriers

<column gap="tiny">

**AC** 13, **Fort** +10, **Ref** +4

**Hardness**
6

**HP**
24 (BT 12)

**Immunities**
object immunities

</column>

**Tempt** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) **Trigger** A creature looks into the mirror; **Effect** The triggering creature sees themselves enjoying their deepest desires in the mirror's reflection, becoming [fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=72) (Will DC 20 negates). This fascination ends automatically when the mirror is broken or covered up.

---
**Reset**
automatic
name: Reflected Desires
